1→# @zazcart/toolkit 2→ 3→[![npm version](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/@zazcart/toolkit.svg)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@zazcart/toolkit) 4→[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-blue.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT) 5→ 6→A TypeScript utility library providing runtime type validation, state management, and common helpers for complex frontend applications including WebGL/game loops requiring high-performance state management. 7→ 8→## Table of Contents 9→ 10→- [Installation](#installation) 11→- [Quick Start](#quick-start) 12→ - [Schema Validation](#schema-validation) 13→ - [State Machine](#state-machine) 14→ - [Helpers](#helpers) 15→- [API Reference](#api-reference) 16→ - [Schema Module](#schema-module) 17→ - [State Machine Module](#state-machine-module) 18→ - [Helpers Module](#helpers-module) 19→- [Advanced Usage](#advanced-usage) 20→- [TypeScript Support](#typescript-support) 21→- [License](#license) 22→ 23→--- 24→ 25→## Installation 26→ 27→```bash 28→npm install @zazcart/toolkit 29→# or 30→pnpm add @zazcart/toolkit 31→# or 32→bun add @zazcart/toolkit 33→``` 34→ 35→**Peer Dependencies:** 36→- `react` >= 18.0.0 37→ 38→**Dependencies (bundled):** 39→- `immer` - Immutable state updates 40→- `date-fns` - Date formatting 41→- `lru-cache` - Schema caching 42→- `typebox` - Runtime type validation 43→ 44→--- 45→ 46→## Quick Start 47→ 48→### Schema Validation 49→ 50→```typescript 51→import { createModel, createType, InferType, isType } from '@zazcart/toolkit'; 52→ 53→// Create a validated model 54→const UserSchema = createModel({ 55→ id: 'number', 56→ name: 'string', 57→ email: 'string?', // optional 58→ roles: 'string[]', // array 59→}); 60→ 61→type User = InferType; 62→ 63→// Parse and validate data 64→const user = UserSchema.parse({ 65→ id: 1, 66→ name: 'John', 67→ roles: ['admin'], 68→}); 69→ 70→// Type guard 71→if (isType(someValue, 'number')) { 72→ console.log(someValue.toFixed(2)); // TypeScript knows it's a number 73→} 74→``` 75→ 76→### State Machine 77→ 78→```typescript 79→import { createMachine, createSlice } from '@zazcart/toolkit'; 80→ 81→// Create a slice 82→const counterSlice = createSlice({ 83→ id: 'counter', 84→ initial: { count: 0 }, 85→ cache: { policy: 'session' }, 86→ reducers: { 87→ increment: (state) => { state.count += 1; }, 88→ decrement: (state) => { state.count -= 1; }, 89→ set: (state, value: number) => { state.count = value; }, 90→ }, 91→}); 92→ 93→// Create the machine 94→const appMachine = createMachine({ 95→ id: 'app', 96→ slices: { counter: counterSlice }, 97→ context: { userID: null }, 98→}); 99→ 100→// Use in React components 101→function Counter() { 102→ const { state, actions } = appMachine.useMachine('counter'); 103→ 104→ return ( 105→
106→ {state.count} 107→ 108→ 109→
110→ ); 111→} 112→``` 113→ 114→### Helpers 115→ 116→```typescript 117→import { 118→ getProperty, 119→ ms, 120→ formatDate, 121→ invariant, 122→ tryCatch, 123→} from '@zazcart/toolkit'; 124→ 125→// Deep property access 126→const street = getProperty(user, 'address.street'); 127→ 128→// Time conversion 129→const timeout = ms('5m'); // 300000 (5 minutes in ms) 130→ 131→// Date formatting 132→const formatted = formatDate(new Date(), 'yyyy-MM-dd'); // '2025-12-28' 133→ 134→// Assertions 135→invariant(user.id, 'User ID is required'); 136→ 137→// Safe error handling 138→const [error, result] = tryCatch(() => JSON.parse(data)); 139→if (error) { 140→ console.error('Parse failed:', error.message); 141→} 142→``` 143→ 144→--- 145→ 146→## API Reference 147→ 148→### Schema Module 149→ 150→The schema module provides runtime type validation using TypeBox under the hood. 151→ 152→#### `createType(definition)` 153→ 154→Creates a compiled type validator from a type definition. String definitions are cached (LRU, 2000 max entries, 1 minute TTL). 155→ 156→```typescript 157→function createType( 158→ definition: Definition 159→): Type> 160→``` 161→ 162→**Parameters:** 163→- `definition` - Type definition (string or object format) 164→ 165→**Returns:** Type wrapper with validation methods 166→ 167→**Example:** 168→```typescript 169→// String definitions (cached) 170→const stringType = createType('string'); 171→const optionalNumber = createType('number?'); 172→const stringArray = createType('string[]'); 173→ 174→// Object definitions 175→const userType = createType({ 176→ properties: { 177→ id: 'number', 178→ name: 'string', 179→ } 180→}); 181→ 182→// Enum 183→const statusType = createType({ 184→ enum: ['draft', 'published', 'archived'] as const 185→}); 186→ 187→// Union 188→const idType = createType({ 189→ union: ['string', 'number'] 190→}); 191→ 192→// Record (map) 193→const configType = createType({ 194→ record: 'string' // Record 195→}); 196→``` 197→ 198→#### `createModel(properties)` 199→ 200→Convenience function for creating object schemas. Equivalent to `createType({ properties })`. 201→ 202→```typescript 203→function createModel( 204→ properties: Schema 205→): Type<{ properties: Schema }, InferType<{ properties: Schema }>> 206→``` 207→ 208→**Example:** 209→```typescript 210→const AddressSchema = createModel({ 211→ street: 'string', 212→ city: 'string', 213→ zip: 'string?', 214→}); 215→ 216→type Address = InferType; 217→// { street: string; city: string; zip?: string } 218→``` 219→ 220→#### Type Wrapper Methods 221→ 222→The returned type wrapper provides these methods: 223→ 224→| Method | Description | 225→|---------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| 226→| `parse(value, path?)` | Validates and returns typed value. Throws on failure. Optional `path` for nested access. | 227→| `parseList(value, path?)` | Validates array of values. Throws on failure. | 228→| `isValid(value)` | Type guard returning boolean. | 229→| `list()` | Returns cached array schema for this type. | 230→ 231→```typescript 232→const UserSchema = createModel({ id: 'number', name: 'string' }); 233→ 234→// Parse single value 235→const user = UserSchema.parse({ id: 1, name: 'John' }); 236→ 237→// Parse from nested path 238→const user = UserSchema.parse(apiResponse, 'data.user'); 239→ 240→// Parse array 241→const users = UserSchema.parseList(apiResponse, 'data.users'); 242→ 243→// Type guard 244→if (UserSchema.isValid(unknown)) { 245→ console.log(unknown.name); // TypeScript knows the type 246→} 247→``` 248→ 249→#### `isType(value, definition)` 250→ 251→Type guard that checks if a value matches a type definition. 252→ 253→```typescript 254→function isType( 255→ value: unknown, 256→ definition: T 257→): value is InferType 258→``` 259→ 260→**Example:** 261→```typescript 262→if (isType(value, 'date')) { 263→ console.log(value.toISOString()); // value is Date 264→} 265→ 266→if (isType(value, 'number?')) { 267→ // value is number | undefined 268→} 269→``` 270→ 271→#### `invariantType(definition, value, parent?, message?)` 272→ 273→Assertion that throws if value doesn't match the type definition. 274→ 275→```typescript 276→function invariantType( 277→ definition: T, 278→ value: unknown, 279→ parentFunction?: Function, 280→ customMessage?: string 281→): asserts value is InferType 282→``` 283→ 284→**Parameters:** 285→- `definition` - Type to validate against 286→- `value` - Value to validate 287→- `parentFunction` - Optional function for stack trace 288→- `customMessage` - Custom error message with replacements: 289→ - `$definition` - The type definition 290→ - `$typename` - Actual type of value 291→ - `$originalMessage` - Original validation error 292→ 293→**Example:** 294→```typescript 295→function processUser(data: unknown) { 296→ invariantType('object', data, processUser); 297→ // data is now typed as object 298→ 299→ invariantType( 300→ { properties: { id: 'number' } }, 301→ data, 302→ processUser, 303→ 'Invalid user: expected $definition, got $typename' 304→ ); 305→} 306→``` 307→ 308→#### Supported Types 309→ 310→| Type | Description | Example | 311→|-------------|---------------------------|--------------| 312→| `string` | String values | `'hello'` | 313→| `number` | Number values | `42` | 314→| `boolean` | Boolean values | `true` | 315→| `null` | Null value | `null` | 316→| `undefined` | Undefined value | `undefined` | 317→| `date` | Valid Date objects | `new Date()` | 318→| `function` | Function values | `() => {}` | 319→| `object` | Generic object | `{}` | 320→| `unknown` | Any value | - | 321→| `any` | Any value (no validation) | - | 322→ 323→**Modifiers:** 324→- `?` suffix - Makes type optional (allows undefined) 325→- `[]` suffix - Makes type an array 326→ 327→**Examples:** 328→```typescript 329→'string?' // string | undefined 330→'number[]' // number[] 331→'string[]?' // string[] | undefined 332→'boolean?[]' // (boolean | undefined)[] 333→``` 334→ 335→#### `InferType` 336→ 337→Utility type to infer TypeScript type from a type definition. 338→ 339→```typescript 340→type User = InferType; 341→ 342→// From string definition 343→type ID = InferType<'number'>; // number 344→ 345→// From object definition 346→type Config = InferType<{ 347→ properties: { debug: 'boolean' } 348→}>; // { debug: boolean } 349→``` 350→ 351→--- 352→ 353→### State Machine Module 354→ 355→A Redux-like state management system with built-in React hooks, Immer integration, and intelligent caching. 356→ 357→#### `createMachine(config)` 358→ 359→Factory function to create a state machine. 360→ 361→```typescript 362→function createMachine( 363→ config: MachineConfig 364→): StateMachine 365→``` 366→ 367→**Configuration Options:** 368→ 369→| Option | Type | Required | Default | Description | 370→|-----------------|--------------------------------|----------|---------|------------------------------------------| 371→| `id` | `string` | Yes | - | Unique identifier for the machine | 372→| `slices` | `Record` | Yes | - | Slice definitions | 373→| `context` | `{ userID, ...}` | No | - | Runtime context with required `userID` | 374→| `selectors` | `Record T>` | No | - | Computed state selectors | 375→| `initial` | `Partial` | No | - | Initial state overrides (for hydration) | 376→| `sessionID` | `string` | No | auto | App session ID for cache invalidation | 377→| `trackMetadata` | `boolean` | No | `false` | Track state metadata (disable for 60fps) | 378→ 379→**Example:** 380→```typescript 381→const appMachine = createMachine({ 382→ id: 'app', 383→ slices: { 384→ counter: counterSlice, 385→ user: userSlice, 386→ }, 387→ context: { 388→ userID: getCurrentUserId(), 389→ }, 390→ selectors: { 391→ totalItems: (state) => state.cart.items.length, 392→ isLoggedIn: (state) => state.user.id !== null, 393→ }, 394→ trackMetadata: false, // For high-frequency updates 395→}); 396→``` 397→ 398→**Returned Machine Properties:** 399→ 400→| Property | Type | Description | 401→|-----------------------|------------------|---------------------------------| 402→| `ID` | `string` | Machine identifier | 403→| `useMachine(path)` | Hook | React hook for slice access | 404→| `useSelector(fn)` | Hook | React hook for state selection | 405→| `use{ID}` | Hook | Aliased useMachine | 406→| `use{ID}Selector` | Hook | Aliased useSelector | 407→| `actions` | `object` | Nested action creators by slice | 408→| `actionTypes` | `object` | Nested action type strings | 409→| `{ID}Actions` | `object` | Flattened action creators | 410→| `{ID}ActionTypes` | `object` | Flattened action types | 411→| `dispatch(action)` | `function` | Dispatch action | 412→| `getState()` | `function` | Get current state | 413→| `subscribe(listener)` | `function` | Subscribe to changes | 414→| `reset()` | `function` | Reset to initial state | 415→| `getContext()` | `function` | Get runtime context | 416→| `provideContext(ctx)` | `function` | Update runtime context | 417→| `invalidations` | `object \| null` | Cache invalidation report | 418→ 419→#### `createSlice(config)` / `createSyncSlice(config)` 420→ 421→Factory for creating synchronous slices with Immer-based reducers. 422→ 423→```typescript 424→function createSlice( 425→ config: SliceConfig 426→): Slice 427→``` 428→ 429→**Configuration Options:** 430→ 431→| Option | Type | Required | Description | 432→|------------|---------------------------------------------|----------|-------------------------| 433→| `id` | `string` | Yes | Unique slice identifier | 434→| `initial` | `State` | Yes | Initial state | 435→| `reducers` | `Record void>` | Yes | Immer-based reducers | 436→| `cache` | `CacheStrategy` | Yes | Cache configuration | 437→ 438→**Example:** 439→```typescript 440→const todoSlice = createSlice({ 441→ id: 'todo', 442→ initial: { 443→ items: [] as string[], 444→ filter: 'all' as 'all' | 'active' | 'completed', 445→ }, 446→ cache: { policy: 'private', ttl: '1h' }, 447→ reducers: { 448→ add: (state, text: string) => { 449→ state.items.push(text); 450→ }, 451→ remove: (state, index: number) => { 452→ state.items.splice(index, 1); 453→ }, 454→ setFilter: (state, filter: 'all' | 'active' | 'completed') => { 455→ state.filter = filter; 456→ }, 457→ clear: (state) => { 458→ state.items = []; 459→ }, 460→ }, 461→}); 462→``` 463→ 464→#### `createAsyncSlice(config)` 465→ 466→Factory for creating asynchronous slices for data fetching. 467→ 468→```typescript 469→function createAsyncSlice( 470→ config: AsyncSliceConfig 471→): AsyncSlice 472→``` 473→ 474→**Configuration Options:** 475→ 476→| Option | Type | Required | Description | 477→|-----------|-----------------------------------------|----------|-------------------------------| 478→| `id` | `string` | Yes | Unique slice identifier | 479→| `load` | `(payload, context) => Promise` | Yes | Async loader function | 480→| `context` | `SliceContext` | Yes | Required context dependencies | 481→| `cache` | `CacheStrategy` | Yes | Cache configuration | 482→| `initial` | `AsyncSliceState` | No | Initial state override | 483→ 484→**Auto-generated State Shape:** 485→```typescript 486→type AsyncSliceState = { 487→ loading: boolean; // Currently loading 488→ loaded: boolean; // Has loaded successfully 489→ failed: boolean; // Last load failed 490→ error: string | null; // Error message 491→ value: Value | null; // Loaded data 492→ changes: number; // State change count 493→} 494→``` 495→ 496→**Auto-generated Actions:** 497→- `load(payload)` - Start loading 498→- `success(data)` - Handle success 499→- `fail(error)` - Handle failure 500→- `clear()` - Reset state 501→ 502→**Example:** 503→```typescript 504→const userSlice = createAsyncSlice({ 505→ id: 'user', 506→ cache: { policy: 'private', ttl: '30m', swr: true }, 507→ context: { 508→ api: {} as ApiClient, 509→ }, 510→ async load(userId: string, { api }) { 511→ return api.getUser(userId); 512→ }, 513→}); 514→ 515→// Usage in component 516→function UserProfile({ userId }: { userId: string }) { 517→ const { state, actions } = appMachine.useMachine('user'); 518→ 519→ useEffect(() => { 520→ actions.load(userId); 521→ }, [userId]); 522→ 523→ if (state.loading) return ; 524→ if (state.failed) return ; 525→ if (!state.loaded) return null; 526→ 527→ return ; 528→} 529→``` 530→ 531→#### Cache Strategy 532→ 533→Configuration for slice caching behavior. 534→ 535→```typescript 536→type CacheStrategy = { 537→ policy: 'public' | 'private' | 'session'; 538→ ttl?: MSTimeString; 539→ swr?: boolean; 540→} 541→``` 542→ 543→**Policies:** 544→ 545→| Policy | Invalidation Trigger | Use Case | 546→|-----------|----------------------|-------------------------------------------| 547→| `public` | TTL only | Global data (prices, feature flags) | 548→| `private` | User change OR TTL | User-specific data (profile, permissions) | 549→| `session` | App restart OR TTL | Screen-specific temporary data | 550→ 551→**Options:** 552→ 553→| Option | Type | Default | Description | 554→|----------|-----------|---------|------------------------------------------------------------| 555→| `policy` | `string` | - | Cache lifecycle policy | 556→| `ttl` | `string` | - | Time-to-live (e.g., `'1h'`, `'30m'`, `'5s'`) | 557→| `swr` | `boolean` | `true` | Stale-While-Revalidate: return stale data while refetching | 558→ 559→**Examples:** 560→```typescript 561→// Public data cached for 1 hour 562→{ policy: 'public', ttl: '1h' } 563→ 564→// User data, invalidated on logout 565→{ policy: 'private' } 566→ 567→// Session data with 5 minute TTL 568→{ policy: 'session', ttl: '5m' } 569→ 570→// Always fresh (TTL = 0) 571→{ policy: 'public', ttl: '0' } 572→``` 573→ 574→#### React Hooks 575→ 576→**`useMachine(path)`** 577→ 578→Access slice state and actions in React components. 579→ 580→```typescript 581→// For slices - returns { state, actions } 582→const { state, actions } = machine.useMachine('counter'); 583→ 584→// For selectors - returns value directly 585→const totalItems = machine.useMachine('totalItems'); 586→``` 587→ 588→**`useSelector(selector)`** 589→ 590→Select specific state with minimal re-renders. 591→ 592→```typescript 593→// Prefer useSelector for frequently updating state 594→const count = machine.useSelector(state => state.counter.count); 595→ 596→// Select derived data 597→const activeItems = machine.useSelector( 598→ state => state.todo.items.filter(i => !i.completed) 599→); 600→``` 601→ 602→#### Action Types 603→ 604→Action type format: `@{machineID}/{sliceID}/{actionName}` 605→ 606→```typescript 607→const machine = createMachine({ 608→ id: 'app', 609→ slices: { counter: counterSlice }, 610→}); 611→ 612→// Access action types 613→machine.actionTypes.counter.increment 614→// => '@app/counter/increment' 615→ 616→// Flattened alias 617→machine.appActionTypes.CounterIncrement 618→// => '@app/counter/increment' 619→``` 620→ 621→#### `CachePolicy` Enum 622→ 623→Exported enum for cache policy values: 624→ 625→```typescript 626→import { CachePolicy } from '@zazcart/toolkit'; 627→ 628→CachePolicy.public // 'public' 629→CachePolicy.private // 'private' 630→CachePolicy.session // 'session' 631→``` 632→ 633→#### Helper Functions 634→ 635→**`createMachineConfig(config)`** 636→ 637→Type-safe config builder (returns the same config, useful for type inference): 638→ 639→```typescript 640→import { createMachineConfig } from '@zazcart/toolkit'; 641→ 642→const config = createMachineConfig({ 643→ id: 'app', 644→ slices: { counter: counterSlice }, 645→ context: { userID: null }, 646→}); 647→``` 648→ 649→**`basicRandomID()`** 650→ 651→Generates a simple random ID (used internally for session IDs): 652→ 653→```typescript 654→import { basicRandomID } from '@zazcart/toolkit'; 655→ 656→const id = basicRandomID(); // e.g., 'm7k9q3x8rabc123def' 657→``` 658→ 659→**`GLOBAL_APP_SESSION_ID`** 660→ 661→Auto-generated session ID for the current app instance: 662→ 663→```typescript 664→import { GLOBAL_APP_SESSION_ID } from '@zazcart/toolkit'; 665→// Unique per app initialization, used for cache invalidation 666→``` 667→ 668→--- 669→ 670→### Helpers Module 671→ 672→Pure utility functions with no side effects. 673→ 674→#### Object Utilities 675→ 676→**`objectKeys(obj)`** 677→```typescript 678→function objectKeys(obj: T): Extract[] 679→``` 680→Type-safe Object.keys. 681→ 682→**`objectEntries(obj)`** 683→```typescript 684→function objectEntries(obj: T): ObjectEntries 685→``` 686→Type-safe Object.entries with proper tuple types. 687→ 688→**`objectValues(obj)`** 689→```typescript 690→function objectValues(obj: T): T[keyof T][] 691→``` 692→Type-safe Object.values. 693→ 694→**`isObject(value)`** 695→```typescript 696→function isObject(value: unknown): value is Record 697→``` 698→Checks if value is a plain object. 699→ 700→**`assertObject(value, message?, parent?)`** 701→```typescript 702→function assertObject( 703→ value: unknown, 704→ message?: string, 705→ parent?: Function 706→): asserts value is Record 707→``` 708→Asserts value is an object, throws otherwise. 709→ 710→**`getProperty(obj, path, allowFalsy?)`** 711→```typescript 712→function getProperty>( 713→ obj: T, 714→ path: P, 715→ allowFalsy?: boolean 716→): PathType 717→``` 718→Deep property access with dot notation. Throws on falsy values unless `allowFalsy` is true. 719→ 720→```typescript 721→const user = { address: { street: '123 Main' } }; 722→const street = getProperty(user, 'address.street'); // '123 Main' 723→const zip = getProperty(user, 'address.zip', true); // undefined (allowed) 724→``` 725→ 726→**`setProperty(obj, path, value)`** 727→```typescript 728→function setProperty>( 729→ obj: T, 730→ path: P, 731→ value: PathType 732→): T 733→``` 734→Deep property setter. 735→ 736→**`keyBy(arr, key, onDuplicate?)`** 737→```typescript 738→function keyBy( 739→ arr: T[], 740→ key: keyof T | ((item: T) => string), 741→ onDuplicate?: (key: string, value: T) => void 742→): Record 743→``` 744→Convert array to keyed object. 745→ 746→```typescript 747→const users = [{ id: 1, name: 'John' }, { id: 2, name: 'Jane' }]; 748→const byId = keyBy(users, 'id'); 749→// { '1': { id: 1, name: 'John' }, '2': { id: 2, name: 'Jane' } } 750→``` 751→ 752→**`reduceObject(obj, reducer)`** 753→```typescript 754→function reduceObject( 755→ obj: O, 756→ reducer: (entry: [keyof O, O[keyof O]]) => Partial 757→): R 758→``` 759→O(N) object reduction using mutation (performant). 760→ 761→**`deepFreeze(obj)`** 762→```typescript 763→function deepFreeze(obj: T): Readonly 764→``` 765→Recursively freeze an object. 766→ 767→**`requireAll(record)`** 768→```typescript 769→function requireAll(record: Partial): T 770→``` 771→Assert all values in record are non-falsy. 772→ 773→**`objectHasOwnProperty(obj, key)`** 774→```typescript 775→function objectHasOwnProperty(obj: T, key: unknown): key is keyof T 776→``` 777→Type-safe hasOwnProperty check. 778→ 779→**`flatRecordRecords(prefix, record)`** 780→```typescript 781→function flatRecordRecords( 782→ prefix: Prefix, 783→ record: Record 784→): FlatRecordRecords 785→``` 786→Flatten nested records with CamelCase keys. 787→ 788→```typescript 789→flatRecordRecords('App', { user: { load: fn } }); 790→// { AppUserLoad: fn } 791→``` 792→ 793→#### Error Utilities 794→ 795→**`invariant(value, message?, parent?)`** 796→```typescript 797→function invariant( 798→ value: unknown, 799→ message?: string | ((typeName: string) => string), 800→ parent?: Function 801→): asserts value 802→``` 803→Assert value is truthy. 804→ 805→```typescript 806→invariant(user.id, 'User ID is required'); 807→invariant(data, typeName => `Expected data, got ${typeName}`); 808→``` 809→ 810→**`InvariantError`** 811→```typescript 812→class InvariantError extends Error { 813→ static logSensitiveData: boolean; 814→ constructor( 815→ message: string | { message: string; stack?: string }, 816→ parentFn: Function, 817→ extraInfo?: any 818→ ) 819→} 820→``` 821→Custom error with stack trace manipulation. 822→ 823→**`tryCatch(handler, parent?)`** 824→```typescript 825→function tryCatch( 826→ handler: () => T, 827→ parent?: Function 828→): readonly [Error, never] | readonly [never, T] 829→``` 830→Safe error handling returning tuple. 831→ 832→```typescript 833→const [error, result] = tryCatch(() => JSON.parse(data)); 834→if (error) { 835→ console.error('Parse failed:', error.message); 836→} else { 837→ console.log('Parsed:', result); 838→} 839→``` 840→ 841→**`moveStackTrace(errorOrFn, newStack)`** 842→```typescript 843→function moveStackTrace(error: Error, newStack: Function): Error 844→function moveStackTrace(fn: () => T, newStack: Function): T 845→``` 846→Relocate error stack trace or wrap function with stack manipulation. 847→ 848→**`createErrorList(prefix?)`** 849→```typescript 850→function createErrorList(prefix?: string): { 851→ add(message: string): void; 852→ check(): void; 853→ getError(): never; 854→} 855→``` 856→Collect multiple errors and throw single combined error. 857→ 858→```typescript 859→const errors = createErrorList('Validation failed: '); 860→if (!name) errors.add('name is required'); 861→if (!email) errors.add('email is required'); 862→errors.check(); // Throws if any errors were added 863→``` 864→ 865→**`isErrorLike(value)` / `assertErrorLike(value)`** 866→```typescript 867→function isErrorLike(value: unknown): value is { message: string; stack: string } 868→function assertErrorLike(value: unknown): asserts value is ErrorLike 869→``` 870→Error type guards. 871→ 872→**`captureStackTrace(error, constructor?)`** 873→```typescript 874→function captureStackTrace(error: Error, constructor?: Function): void 875→``` 876→Cross-platform (Node.js/Bun) stack trace capture. 877→ 878→#### String Utilities 879→ 880→**`isString(value)`** 881→```typescript 882→function isString(value: unknown): value is string 883→``` 884→String type guard. 885→ 886→**`assertString(value, options?)`** 887→```typescript 888→function assertString( 889→ value: unknown, 890→ options?: { notEmpty?: boolean; message?: string } 891→): asserts value is string 892→``` 893→Assert value is string, optionally non-empty. 894→ 895→```typescript 896→assertString(name); 897→assertString(name, { notEmpty: true, message: 'Name is required' }); 898→``` 899→ 900→**`capitalize(value)`** 901→```typescript 902→function capitalize(value: T): Capitalize 903→``` 904→Capitalize first letter. 905→ 906→```typescript 907→capitalize('hello'); // 'Hello' 908→``` 909→ 910→#### Time Utilities 911→ 912→**`ms(timeString)`** 913→```typescript 914→function ms(timeString: MSTimeString): number 915→``` 916→Convert time string to milliseconds. 917→ 918→| Unit | Description | Example | 919→|-------------------------|--------------|-----------| 920→| `y` | Years | `'1y'` | 921→| `mo` / `mon` / `months` | Months | `'6mo'` | 922→| `w` | Weeks | `'2w'` | 923→| `d` | Days | `'7d'` | 924→| `h` | Hours | `'24h'` | 925→| `m` / `min` | Minutes | `'30m'` | 926→| `s` | Seconds | `'60s'` | 927→| `ms` | Milliseconds | `'500ms'` | 928→ 929→```typescript 930→ms('1s'); // 1000 931→ms('5m'); // 300000 932→ms('1h'); // 3600000 933→ms('1d'); // 86400000 934→ms(500); // 500 (number passthrough) 935→``` 936→ 937→**`delay(time?)`** 938→```typescript 939→function delay(time?: number): Promise 940→``` 941→Promise-based delay (default 500ms). 942→ 943→```typescript 944→await delay(1000); // Wait 1 second 945→await delay(); // Wait 500ms 946→``` 947→ 948→#### Date Utilities 949→ 950→**`formatDate(date, pattern, options?)`** 951→```typescript 952→function formatDate( 953→ date: Date | string | number, 954→ pattern: string, 955→ options?: { locale?: Locale } 956→): string 957→``` 958→Format dates using date-fns patterns. 959→ 960→```typescript 961→formatDate(new Date(), 'yyyy-MM-dd'); // '2025-12-28' 962→formatDate(new Date(), 'MMMM dd, yyyy'); // 'December 28, 2025' 963→formatDate('2025-01-15', 'MMM dd'); // 'Jan 15' 964→``` 965→ 966→**`setDefaultDateLocale(locale)` / `getDefaultDateLocale()`** 967→```typescript 968→function setDefaultDateLocale(locale: Locale | null): void 969→function getDefaultDateLocale(): Locale | null 970→``` 971→Set/get global date locale. 972→ 973→```typescript 974→import { ptBR } from 'date-fns/locale'; 975→setDefaultDateLocale(ptBR); 976→formatDate(new Date(), 'MMMM'); // 'dezembro' 977→``` 978→ 979→#### Other Utilities 980→ 981→**`createProxy(thunk, target?)`** 982→```typescript 983→function createProxy(thunk: () => T, target?: object): T 984→``` 985→Lazy initialization proxy. Thunk executed on first property access. 986→ 987→```typescript 988→const config = createProxy(() => { 989→ console.log('Initializing...'); 990→ return loadExpensiveConfig(); 991→}); 992→// 'Initializing...' only prints when config is first accessed 993→``` 994→ 995→**`maskData(value)`** 996→```typescript 997→function maskData(value: any): object 998→``` 999→Replace leaf values with type names for safe logging. 1000→ 1001→```typescript 1002→maskData({ id: 123, email: 'john@example.com' }); 1003→// { id: 'Number', email: 'String' } 1004→``` 1005→ 1006→**`getTypeOf(value)`** 1007→```typescript 1008→function getTypeOf(value: any): string 1009→``` 1010→Human-readable type name. 1011→ 1012→```typescript 1013→getTypeOf(null); // 'Array' 1020→getTypeOf(() => {}); // 'Function' 1021→``` 1022→ 1023→**`EMPTY`** 1024→```typescript 1025→const EMPTY: unique symbol 1026→``` 1027→Unique sentinel value to distinguish from `undefined`/`null`. 1028→ 1029→--- 1030→ 1031→## Advanced Usage 1032→ 1033→### High-Frequency Machines (60fps) 1034→ 1035→For game loops or high-frequency updates, disable metadata tracking: 1036→ 1037→```typescript 1038→const gameMachine = createMachine({ 1039→ id: 'game', 1040→ trackMetadata: false, // Critical: avoids GC thrashing 1041→ slices: { 1042→ player: createSlice({ 1043→ id: 'player', 1044→ initial: { x: 0, y: 0 }, 1045→ cache: { policy: 'session' }, 1046→ reducers: { 1047→ move: (state, { x, y }: { x: number; y: number }) => { 1048→ state.x = x; 1049→ state.y = y; 1050→ }, 1051→ }, 1052→ }), 1053→ }, 1054→ context: { userID: null }, 1055→}); 1056→``` 1057→ 1058→### Custom Selectors 1059→ 1060→Compute derived state efficiently: 1061→ 1062→```typescript 1063→const shopMachine = createMachine({ 1064→ id: 'shop', 1065→ slices: { cart: cartSlice, products: productsSlice }, 1066→ selectors: { 1067→ cartTotal: (state) => 1068→ state.cart.items.reduce((sum, item) => { 1069→ const product = state.products.byId[item.productId]; 1070→ return sum + (product?.price ?? 0) * item.quantity; 1071→ }, 0), 1072→ itemCount: (state) => 1073→ state.cart.items.reduce((sum, item) => sum + item.quantity, 0), 1074→ }, 1075→ context: { userID: null }, 1076→}); 1077→ 1078→// Access directly 1079→function CartSummary() { 1080→ const total = shopMachine.useMachine('cartTotal'); 1081→ const count = shopMachine.useMachine('itemCount'); 1082→ return
{count} items: ${total}
; 1083→} 1084→``` 1085→ 1086→### Cache Invalidation Handling 1087→ 1088→Handle stale data after cache invalidation: 1089→ 1090→```typescript 1091→const machine = createMachine({ 1092→ id: 'app', 1093→ slices: { user: userSlice }, 1094→ initial: cachedState, // Hydrated from storage 1095→ context: { userID: currentUserId }, 1096→}); 1097→ 1098→// Check for invalidations 1099→if (machine.invalidations) { 1100→ const { stale, reset } = machine.invalidations; 1101→ 1102→ // Slices that need refetching (SWR mode) 1103→ if (stale.length) { 1104→ stale.forEach(sliceKey => { 1105→ machine.actions[sliceKey]?.load?.(); 1106→ }); 1107→ } 1108→ 1109→ // Slices that were fully reset 1110→ console.log('Reset slices:', reset); 1111→} 1112→``` 1113→ 1114→### Context Management 1115→ 1116→Provide runtime dependencies to slices: 1117→ 1118→```typescript 1119→const appMachine = createMachine({ 1120→ id: 'app', 1121→ slices: { data: dataSlice }, 1122→ context: { 1123→ userID: null, 1124→ api: apiClient, 1125→ analytics: analyticsService, 1126→ }, 1127→}); 1128→ 1129→// Update context after login 1130→function onLogin(user: User) { 1131→ appMachine.provideContext({ 1132→ userID: user.id, 1133→ }); 1134→} 1135→``` 1136→ 1137→--- 1138→ 1139→## TypeScript Support 1140→ 1141→### Type Inference 1142→ 1143→All APIs are fully typed with automatic inference: 1144→ 1145→```typescript 1146→// Schema type inference 1147→const UserSchema = createModel({ 1148→ id: 'number', 1149→ name: 'string', 1150→ email: 'string?', 1151→ roles: 'string[]', 1152→}); 1153→ 1154→type User = InferType; 1155→// { id: number; name: string; email?: string; roles: string[] } 1156→ 1157→// Machine state inference 1158→type AppState = InferMachineState; 1159→``` 1160→ 1161→### Utility Types 1162→ 1163→Available type utilities from `@zazcart/toolkit`: 1164→ 1165→| Type | Description | 1166→|--------------------------|------------------------------------| 1167→| `Writable` | Remove readonly modifiers | 1168→| `Compute` | Flatten intersection types | 1169→| `Paths` | All dot-notation paths of object | 1170→| `PathType` | Type at specific path | 1171→| `Falsy` | Union of falsy values | 1172→| `UnionToIntersection` | Convert union to intersection | 1173→| `NullableToPartial` | Convert nullable props to optional | 1174→| `Serializable` | JSON-serializable types | 1175→| `AnyFunction` | Generic function type | 1176→| `PromiseValue` | Extract resolved type from Promise | 1177→ 1178→--- 1179→ 1180→## License 1181→ 1182→MIT 1183→ Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. # HQ - Harness Orchestrator & Dashboard

## Visão Geral

**HQ** é uma aplicação desktop nativa (Tauri + React + BlueprintJS v6) que substitui o terminal para gerenciamento de desenvolvimento com AI agents. Implementa o padrão **Anthropic Harness** para trabalho de longa duração em múltiplas sessões.

### O que é um Harness?

> **Problema:** Cada nova sessão de AI começa com ZERO memória do que aconteceu antes.
> **Solução:** Arquivos de estado (`progress.txt`, `features.json`) persistem contexto entre sessões.

**Fonte:** [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents) - Anthropic Engineering, Nov 2025

---

## Stack Tecnológico

| Camada | Tecnologia | Versão | Justificativa |
|--------|-----------|--------|---------------|
| **Runtime** | Tauri | 2.x | App nativo, leve, Rust backend |
| **Frontend** | React | 18.3.x | BlueprintJS 6 requer React 18 |
| **UI** | BlueprintJS | 6.x | Enterprise UI, Kanban components |
| **State** | @zazcart/toolkit | local | `createMachine`, validação, helpers |
| **Build** | Vite | 5.x | Fast HMR, Tauri plugin |
| **Package** | Bun | 1.2+ | Workspace, scripts |

**Dependência crítica:**
```json
"@zazcart/toolkit": "file:/usr/local/packages/toolkit"
```

---

## Estrutura do Projeto

```
~/antonio/HQ/
├── .claude/
│   ├── hooks/                    # Hooks reutilizados do MMS
│   │   ├── session-start.sh      # Context injection
│   │   ├── after-write.sh        # Contextual warnings
│   │   ├── end-of-turn-check.sh  # Quality gate
│   │   └── protect-package-json.sh
│   ├── settings.json             # Hook configuration
│   └── settings.local.json       # Permissions
│
├── .harness/
│   ├── progress.txt              # Session history
│   └── features.json             # Features com status
│
├── src-tauri/                    # Rust backend
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── main.rs               # Entry point
│   │   ├── commands/             # Tauri commands
│   │   │   ├── harness.rs        # Read/write harness files
│   │   │   ├── claude.rs         # Spawn Claude sessions
│   │   │   └── logs.rs           # Real-time log streaming
│   │   └── lib.rs
│   ├── Cargo.toml
│   └── tauri.conf.json
│
├── src/                          # React frontend
│   ├── main.tsx                  # Entry point
│   ├── App.tsx                   # Root component
│   ├── state/                    # @zazcart/toolkit machine
│   │   ├── machine.ts            # Main state machine
│   │   ├── slices/
│   │   │   ├── harness.ts        # Harness state (features, progress)
│   │   │   ├── sessions.ts       # Active Claude sessions
│   │   │   └── logs.ts           # Real-time logs
│   │   └── index.ts
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── KanbanBoard.tsx       # Feature cards (pending/in_progress/done)
│   │   ├── SessionPanel.tsx      # Active sessions list
│   │   ├── LogViewer.tsx         # Real-time log stream
│   │   ├── FeatureCard.tsx       # Individual feature card
│   │   └── HarnessSelector.tsx   # Select project/harness
│   ├── hooks/
│   │   └── useTauriEvents.ts     # Tauri event subscriptions
│   └── styles/
│       └── index.scss            # BlueprintJS overrides
│
├── index.html
├── vite.config.ts
├── package.json
├── CLAUDE.md                     # Project instructions
└── tsconfig.json
```

---

## Features (MVP)

### F001: Project Scaffolding
- Tauri 2.x + React 18 + BlueprintJS 6 + Vite
- @zazcart/toolkit integration
- Basic window with dark theme

### F002: Harness State Machine
```typescript
import { createMachine, createSlice, createAsyncSlice } from '@zazcart/toolkit';

const harnessSlice = createAsyncSlice({
  id: 'harness',
  cache: { policy: 'session' },
  context: { tauriInvoke: {} as typeof invoke },
  async load(projectPath: string, { tauriInvoke }) {
    const features = await tauriInvoke('read_features', { path: projectPath });
    const progress = await tauriInvoke('read_progress', { path: projectPath });
    return { features, progress };
  },
});

const hqMachine = createMachine({
  id: 'hq',
  slices: { harness: harnessSlice, sessions: sessionsSlice, logs: logsSlice },
  context: { userID: null },
  trackMetadata: false, // Performance for logs
});
```

### F003: Kanban Board UI
- 3 colunas: `pending` | `in_progress` | `completed`
- BlueprintJS Card components
- Drag-and-drop (muda status em features.json)
- Color coding por categoria (core, ui, polish)

### F004: Tauri Commands (Rust)
```rust
#[tauri::command]
fn read_features(path: String) -> Result<Vec<Feature>, String> {
    let content = fs::read_to_string(format!("{}/.harness/features.json", path))?;
    serde_json::from_str(&content).map_err(|e| e.to_string())
}

#[tauri::command]
fn spawn_claude_session(harness_path: String, feature_id: String) -> Result<u32, String> {
    // Spawn `claude` CLI with isolation prompt
}

#[tauri::command]
fn watch_logs(session_id: u32, window: Window) -> Result<(), String> {
    // Stream logs via Tauri events
}
```

### F005: Real-time Log Viewer
- WebSocket/Tauri events do processo Claude
- Scroll automático
- Syntax highlighting para code blocks
- Filter por level (info/warn/error)

### F006: Session Management
- Start/Stop Claude sessions
- Ver sessão ativa por feature
- Kill session button

---

## Schemas (@zazcart/toolkit)

```typescript
import { createModel, InferType } from '@zazcart/toolkit';

export const FeatureSchema = createModel({
  id: 'string',
  category: 'string',
  priority: 'number',
  description: 'string',
  passes: 'boolean',
  blockedBy: 'string?',
  steps: 'string[]?',
  notes: 'string?',
});

export type Feature = InferType<typeof FeatureSchema>;

export const SessionSchema = createModel({
  id: 'string',
  featureId: 'string',
  pid: 'number',
  status: { enum: ['running', 'stopped', 'completed', 'failed'] as const },
  startedAt: 'date',
  logs: 'string[]',
});

export type Session = InferType<typeof SessionSchema>;
```

---

## Hooks Reutilizados do MMS

| Hook | Reusar? | Adaptação |
|------|---------|-----------|
| `session-start.sh` | ✅ | Remover refs específicas do MMS |
| `after-write.sh` | ✅ | Generalizar avisos |
| `end-of-turn-check.sh` | ✅ | Remover typecheck específico |
| `protect-package-json.sh` | ✅ | Sem alteração |
| `task-type-detector.py` | ❌ | Muito específico ao MMS |

---

## Plano de Implementação

### Fase 1: Scaffolding (F001)

1. **Criar diretório e iniciar projeto:**
   ```bash
   mkdir -p ~/antonio/HQ
   cd ~/antonio/HQ
   bun create tauri-app . --template react-ts
   ```

2. **Configurar BlueprintJS 6 + toolkit:**
   ```bash
   cd ~/antonio/HQ
   bun add @blueprintjs/core@6 @blueprintjs/icons@6
   bun add "@zazcart/toolkit@file:/usr/local/packages/toolkit"
   ```

3. **Copiar hooks do MMS:**
   ```bash
   cp -r /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/system/.claude ~/antonio/HQ/
   # Adaptar hooks para serem genéricos
   ```

4. **Criar .harness/ inicial:**
   ```bash
   mkdir -p ~/antonio/HQ/.harness
   # Criar features.json inicial com F001-F006
   # Criar progress.txt vazio
   ```

### Fase 2: Core State (F002)

1. Implementar `src/state/machine.ts` com @zazcart/toolkit
2. Criar slices: harness, sessions, logs
3. Definir schemas com createModel

### Fase 3: UI (F003)

1. Kanban board com BlueprintJS Cards
2. Feature cards com status visual
3. Dark theme integration

### Fase 4: Tauri Backend (F004-F006)

1. Rust commands para ler/escrever harness files
2. Spawn Claude CLI subprocess
3. Event streaming para logs real-time

---

## Arquivos Críticos a Criar

| Arquivo | Propósito |
|---------|-----------|
| `~/antonio/HQ/package.json` | Deps: React 18, BlueprintJS 6, toolkit |
| `~/antonio/HQ/src-tauri/Cargo.toml` | Deps Rust: serde_json, tokio |
| `~/antonio/HQ/src/state/machine.ts` | State machine principal |
| `~/antonio/HQ/.harness/features.json` | Features iniciais |
| `~/antonio/HQ/CLAUDE.md` | Instruções do projeto |
| `~/antonio/HQ/.claude/hooks/*` | Hooks adaptados |

---

## Próximos Passos Após Aprovação

1. Criar estrutura de diretórios
2. Iniciar projeto Tauri com template React
3. Instalar dependências (BlueprintJS 6, toolkit)
4. Copiar e adaptar hooks
5. Criar .harness/ com features iniciais
6. Iniciar harness (primeira sessão documenta setup)

│   │   └── useTauriEvents.ts     # Tauri event subscriptions
│   └── styles/
│       └── index.scss            # BlueprintJS overrides
│
├── index.html
├── vite.config.ts
├── package.json
├── CLAUDE.md                     # Project instructions
└── tsconfig.json
```

---

## Features (MVP)

### F001: Project Scaffolding
- Tauri 2.x + React 18 + BlueprintJS 6 + Vite
- @zazcart/toolkit integration
- Basic window with dark theme

### F002: Harness State Machine
```typescript
import { createMachine, createSlice, createAsyncSlice } from '@zazcart/toolkit';

const harnessSlice = createAsyncSlice({
  id: 'harness',
  cache: { policy: 'session' },
  context: { tauriInvoke: {} as typeof invoke },
  async load(projectPath: string, { tauriInvoke }) {
    const features = await tauriInvoke('read_features', { path: projectPath });
    const progress = await tauriInvoke('read_progress', { path: projectPath });
    return { features, progress };
  },
});

const hqMachine = createMachine({
  id: 'hq',
  slices: { harness: harnessSlice, sessions: sessionsSlice, logs: logsSlice },
  context: { userID: null },
  trackMetadata: false, // Performance for logs
});
```

### F003: Kanban Board UI
- 3 colunas: `pending` | `in_progress` | `completed`
- BlueprintJS Card components
- Drag-and-drop (muda status em features.json)
- Color coding por categoria (core, ui, polish)

### F004: Tauri Commands (Rust)
```rust
#[tauri::command]
fn read_features(path: String) -> Result<Vec<Feature>, String> {
    let content = fs::read_to_string(format!("{}/.harness/features.json", path))?;
    serde_json::from_str(&content).map_err(|e| e.to_string())
}

#[tauri::command]
fn spawn_claude_session(harness_path: String, feature_id: String) -> Result<u32, String> {
    // Spawn `claude` CLI with isolation prompt
}

#[tauri::command]
fn watch_logs(session_id: u32, window: Window) -> Result<(), String> {
    // Stream logs via Tauri events
}
```

### F005: Real-time Log Viewer
- WebSocket/Tauri events do processo Claude
- Scroll automático
- Syntax highlighting para code blocks
- Filter por level (info/warn/error)

### F006: Session Management
- Start/Stop Claude sessions
- Ver sessão ativa por feature
- Kill session button

---

## Schemas (@zazcart/toolkit)

```typescript
import { createModel, InferType } from '@zazcart/toolkit';

export const FeatureSchema = createModel({
  id: 'string',
  category: 'string',
  priority: 'number',
  description: 'string',
  passes: 'boolean',
  blockedBy: 'string?',
  steps: 'string[]?',
  notes: 'string?',
});

export type Feature = InferType<typeof FeatureSchema>;

export const SessionSchema = createModel({
  id: 'string',
  featureId: 'string',
  pid: 'number',
  status: { enum: ['running', 'stopped', 'completed', 'failed'] as const },
  startedAt: 'date',
  logs: 'string[]',
});

export type Session = InferType<typeof SessionSchema>;
```

---

## Hooks Reutilizados do MMS

| Hook | Reusar? | Adaptação |
|------|---------|-----------|
| `session-start.sh` | ✅ | Remover refs específicas do MMS |
| `after-write.sh` | ✅ | Generalizar avisos |
| `end-of-turn-check.sh` | ✅ | Remover typecheck específico |
| `protect-package-json.sh` | ✅ | Sem alteração |
| `task-type-detector.py` | ❌ | Muito específico ao MMS |

---

## Plano de Implementação

### Fase 1: Scaffolding (F001)

1. **Criar diretório e iniciar projeto:**
   ```bash
   mkdir -p ~/antonio/HQ
   cd ~/antonio/HQ
   bun create tauri-app . --template react-ts
   ```

2. **Configurar BlueprintJS 6 + toolkit:**

   cd ~/antonio/HQ
   bun add @blueprintjs/core@6 @blueprintjs/icons@6
   bun add "@zazcart/toolkit@file:/usr/local/packages/toolkit"
   ```

3. **Copiar hooks do MMS:**

   ```

4. **Criar .harness/ inicial:**
   ```bash
   mkdir -p ~/antonio/HQ/.harness
   # Criar features.json inicial com F001-F006

   ```

### Fase 2: Core State (F002)

1. Implementar `src/state/machine.ts` com @zazcart/toolkit
2. Criar slices: harness, sessions, logs
3. Definir schemas com createModel

### Fase 3: UI (F003)

1. Kanban board com BlueprintJS Cards
2. Feature cards com status visual
3. Dark theme integration

### Fase 4: Tauri Backend (F004-F006)

1. Rust commands para ler/escrever harness files
2. Spawn Claude CLI subprocess
3. Event streaming para logs real-time

content.content:
# Tauri Terminal Architecture Analysis

## Project Overview
This repository demonstrates building a terminal emulator within the Tauri framework, combining frontend and backend components for PTY (pseudo-terminal) management.

## Core Libraries & Technologies

**Frontend:**
- `xterm.js` - Provides the terminal UI rendering and user interaction handling

**Backend:**
- `portable-pty` - Handles cross-platform pseudo-terminal creation and management
- Rust backend communicates with the Tauri IPC bridge

**Framework:**
- Tauri - Desktop application framework enabling Rust backend + web frontend architecture

## Architecture Pattern

The implementation follows Tauri's standard model:
- **Frontend layer** (TypeScript/HTML): `xterm.js` displays terminal output
- **IPC bridge**: Tauri's invoke system passes commands between frontend/backend
- **Backend layer** (Rust): `portable-pty` manages actual shell processes and I/O

## Key File Structure

```
src-tauri/          → Rust backend code
src/                → TypeScript/JavaScript frontend
index.html          → Entry point
package.json        → Node dependencies
vite.config.ts      → Frontend build configuration
```

## Implementation Notes

The project uses **Jetbrains Mono font** by default (customizable). The architecture emphasizes bidirectional communication: terminal input flows from frontend to backend process, while PTY output streams back to the xterm display component.

**Status:** The creator indicates performance optimization opportunities remain.

# HQ - Harness Orchestrator & Dashboard

## Visão Geral

**HQ** é uma aplicação desktop nativa (Tauri + React + BlueprintJS v6) que substitui o terminal para gerenciamento de desenvolvimento com AI agents. Implementa o padrão **Anthropic Harness** para trabalho de longa duração em múltiplas sessões.

### O que é um Harness?

> **Problema:** Cada nova sessão de AI começa com ZERO memória do que aconteceu antes.
> **Solução:** Arquivos de estado (`progress.txt`, `features.json`) persistem contexto entre sessões.

**Fonte:** [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents) - Anthropic Engineering, Nov 2025

---

## Stack Tecnológico

| Camada | Tecnologia | Versão | Justificativa |
|--------|-----------|--------|---------------|
| **Runtime** | Tauri | 2.x | App nativo, leve, Rust backend |
| **Frontend** | React | 18.3.x | BlueprintJS 6 requer React 18 |
| **UI** | BlueprintJS | 6.x | Enterprise UI, Kanban components |
| **State** | @zazcart/toolkit | local | `createMachine`, validação, helpers |
| **Build** | Vite | 5.x | Fast HMR, Tauri plugin |
| **Package** | Bun | 1.2+ | Workspace, scripts |

**Dependência crítica:**
```json
"@zazcart/toolkit": "file:/usr/local/packages/toolkit"
```

# HQ - Harness Orchestrator & Dashboard

## Visão Geral

**HQ** é uma aplicação desktop nativa **global** (Tauri + React + BlueprintJS v6) que substitui o terminal para gerenciamento de desenvolvimento com AI agents. Funciona como o Claude Code - pode ser executado em qualquer pasta para gerenciar harnesses.

### O que é um Harness?

> **Problema:** Cada nova sessão de AI começa com ZERO memória do que aconteceu antes.
> **Solução:** Arquivos de estado (`progress.txt`, `features.json`) persistem contexto entre sessões.

**Fonte:** [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents) - Anthropic Engineering, Nov 2025

---

## Stack Tecnológico

| Camada | Tecnologia | Versão | Justificativa |
|--------|-----------|--------|---------------|
| **Runtime** | Tauri | 2.x | App nativo, leve, Rust backend |
| **Frontend** | React | 18.3.x | BlueprintJS 6 requer React 18 |
| **UI** | BlueprintJS | 6.x | Enterprise UI, Kanban components |
| **Terminal** | xterm.js | 5.x | Terminal emulator (frontend) |
| **PTY** | portable-pty | Rust | PTY management (backend) |
| **State** | @zazcart/toolkit | local | `createMachine`, validação, helpers |
| **Build** | Vite | 5.x | Fast HMR, Tauri plugin |
| **Package** | Bun | 1.2+ | Workspace, scripts |

**Dependências críticas:**
```json
// package.json
"@zazcart/toolkit": "file:/usr/local/packages/toolkit",
"@xterm/xterm": "^5.5.0",
"@xterm/addon-fit": "^0.10.0"
```

```toml
# Cargo.toml
portable-pty = "0.8"
```

# HQ - Harness Orchestrator & Dashboard

## Visão Geral

**HQ** é uma aplicação desktop nativa (Tauri + React + BlueprintJS v6) que substitui o terminal para gerenciamento de desenvolvimento com AI agents. Implementa o padrão **Anthropic Harness** para trabalho de longa duração em múltiplas sessões.

### O que é um Harness?

> **Problema:** Cada nova sessão de AI começa com ZERO memória do que aconteceu antes.
> **Solução:** Arquivos de estado (`progress.txt`, `features.json`) persistem contexto entre sessões.

**Fonte:** [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents) - Anthropic Engineering, Nov 2025

---

## Stack Tecnológico

| Camada | Tecnologia | Versão | Justificativa |
|--------|-----------|--------|---------------|
| **Runtime** | Tauri | 2.x | App nativo, leve, Rust backend |
| **Frontend** | React | 18.3.x | BlueprintJS 6 requer React 18 |
| **UI** | BlueprintJS | 6.x | Enterprise UI, Kanban components |
| **State** | @zazcart/toolkit | local | `createMachine`, validação, helpers |
| **Build** | Vite | 5.x | Fast HMR, Tauri plugin |
| **Package** | Bun | 1.2+ | Workspace, scripts |

**Dependência crítica:**
```json
"@zazcart/toolkit": "file:/usr/local/packages/toolkit"
```

# HQ - Harness Orchestrator & Dashboard

## Visão Geral

**HQ** é uma aplicação desktop nativa (Tauri + React + BlueprintJS v6) que substitui o terminal para gerenciamento de desenvolvimento com AI agents. Implementa o padrão **Anthropic Harness** para trabalho de longa duração em múltiplas sessões.

### O que é um Harness?

> **Problema:** Cada nova sessão de AI começa com ZERO memória do que aconteceu antes.
> **Solução:** Arquivos de estado (`progress.txt`, `features.json`) persistem contexto entre sessões.

**Fonte:** [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents) - Anthropic Engineering, Nov 2025

---

## Stack Tecnológico

| Camada | Tecnologia | Versão | Justificativa |
|--------|-----------|--------|---------------|
| **Runtime** | Tauri | 2.x | App nativo, leve, Rust backend |
| **Frontend** | React | 18.3.x | BlueprintJS 6 requer React 18 |
| **UI** | BlueprintJS | 6.x | Enterprise UI, Kanban components |
| **State** | @zazcart/toolkit | local | `createMachine`, validação, helpers |
| **Build** | Vite | 5.x | Fast HMR, Tauri plugin |
| **Package** | Bun | 1.2+ | Workspace, scripts |

**Dependência crítica:**
```json
"@zazcart/toolkit": "file:/usr/local/packages/toolkit"
```

---

## Estrutura do Projeto

```
~/antonio/HQ/
├── .claude/
│   ├── hooks/                    # Hooks reutilizados do MMS
│   │   ├── session-start.sh      # Context injection
│   │   ├── after-write.sh        # Contextual warnings
│   │   ├── end-of-turn-check.sh  # Quality gate
│   │   └── protect-package-json.sh
│   ├── settings.json             # Hook configuration
│   └── settings.local.json       # Permissions
│
├── .harness/
│   ├── progress.txt              # Session history
│   └── features.json             # Features com status
│
├── src-tauri/                    # Rust backend
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── main.rs               # Entry point
│   │   ├── commands/             # Tauri commands
│   │   │   ├── harness.rs        # Read/write harness files
│   │   │   ├── claude.rs         # Spawn Claude sessions
│   │   │   └── logs.rs           # Real-time log streaming
│   │   └── lib.rs
│   ├── Cargo.toml
│   └── tauri.conf.json
│
├── src/                          # React frontend
│   ├── main.tsx                  # Entry point
│   ├── App.tsx                   # Root component
│   ├── state/                    # @zazcart/toolkit machine
│   │   ├── machine.ts            # Main state machine
│   │   ├── slices/
│   │   │   ├── harness.ts        # Harness state (features, progress)
│   │   │   ├── sessions.ts       # Active Claude sessions
│   │   │   └── logs.ts           # Real-time logs
│   │   └── index.ts
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── KanbanBoard.tsx       # Feature cards (pending/in_progress/done)
│   │   ├── SessionPanel.tsx      # Active sessions list
│   │   ├── LogViewer.tsx         # Real-time log stream
│   │   ├── FeatureCard.tsx       # Individual feature card
│   │   └── HarnessSelector.tsx   # Select project/harness
│   ├── hooks/
│   │   └── useTauriEvents.ts     # Tauri event subscriptions
│   └── styles/
│       └── index.scss            # BlueprintJS overrides
│
├── index.html
├── vite.config.ts
├── package.json
├── CLAUDE.md                     # Project instructions
└── tsconfig.json
```

---

## Features (MVP)

### F001: Project Scaffolding
- Tauri 2.x + React 18 + BlueprintJS 6 + Vite
- @zazcart/toolkit integration
- Basic window with dark theme

### F002: Harness State Machine
```typescript
import { createMachine, createSlice, createAsyncSlice } from '@zazcart/toolkit';

const harnessSlice = createAsyncSlice({
  id: 'harness',
  cache: { policy: 'session' },
  context: { tauriInvoke: {} as typeof invoke },
  async load(projectPath: string, { tauriInvoke }) {
    const features = await tauriInvoke('read_features', { path: projectPath });
    const progress = await tauriInvoke('read_progress', { path: projectPath });
    return { features, progress };
  },
});

const hqMachine = createMachine({
  id: 'hq',
  slices: { harness: harnessSlice, sessions: sessionsSlice, logs: logsSlice },
  context: { userID: null },
  trackMetadata: false, // Performance for logs
});
```

### F003: Kanban Board UI
- 3 colunas: `pending` | `in_progress` | `completed`
- BlueprintJS Card components
- Drag-and-drop (muda status em features.json)
- Color coding por categoria (core, ui, polish)

### F004: Tauri Commands (Rust)
```rust
#[tauri::command]
fn read_features(path: String) -> Result<Vec<Feature>, String> {
    let content = fs::read_to_string(format!("{}/.harness/features.json", path))?;
    serde_json::from_str(&content).map_err(|e| e.to_string())
}

#[tauri::command]
fn spawn_claude_session(harness_path: String, feature_id: String) -> Result<u32, String> {
    // Spawn `claude` CLI with isolation prompt
}

#[tauri::command]
fn watch_logs(session_id: u32, window: Window) -> Result<(), String> {
    // Stream logs via Tauri events
}
```

### F005: Real-time Log Viewer
- WebSocket/Tauri events do processo Claude
- Scroll automático
- Syntax highlighting para code blocks
- Filter por level (info/warn/error)

### F006: Session Management
- Start/Stop Claude sessions
- Ver sessão ativa por feature
- Kill session button

---

## Schemas (@zazcart/toolkit)

```typescript
import { createModel, InferType } from '@zazcart/toolkit';

export const FeatureSchema = createModel({
  id: 'string',
  category: 'string',
  priority: 'number',
  description: 'string',
  passes: 'boolean',
  blockedBy: 'string?',
  steps: 'string[]?',
  notes: 'string?',
});

export type Feature = InferType<typeof FeatureSchema>;

export const SessionSchema = createModel({
  id: 'string',
  featureId: 'string',
  pid: 'number',
  status: { enum: ['running', 'stopped', 'completed', 'failed'] as const },
  startedAt: 'date',
  logs: 'string[]',
});

export type Session = InferType<typeof SessionSchema>;
```

---

## Hooks Reutilizados do MMS

| Hook | Reusar? | Adaptação |
|------|---------|-----------|
| `session-start.sh` | ✅ | Remover refs específicas do MMS |
| `after-write.sh` | ✅ | Generalizar avisos |
| `end-of-turn-check.sh` | ✅ | Remover typecheck específico |
| `protect-package-json.sh` | ✅ | Sem alteração |
| `task-type-detector.py` | ❌ | Muito específico ao MMS |

---

## Plano de Implementação

### Fase 1: Scaffolding (F001)

1. **Criar diretório e iniciar projeto:**
   ```bash
   mkdir -p ~/antonio/HQ
   cd ~/antonio/HQ
   bun create tauri-app . --template react-ts
   ```

2. **Configurar BlueprintJS 6 + toolkit:**
   ```bash
   cd ~/antonio/HQ
   bun add @blueprintjs/core@6 @blueprintjs/icons@6
   bun add "@zazcart/toolkit@file:/usr/local/packages/toolkit"
   ```

3. **Copiar hooks do MMS:**
   ```bash
   cp -r /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/system/.claude ~/antonio/HQ/
   # Adaptar hooks para serem genéricos
   ```

4. **Criar .harness/ inicial:**
   ```bash
   mkdir -p ~/antonio/HQ/.harness
   # Criar features.json inicial com F001-F006
   # Criar progress.txt vazio
   ```

### Fase 2: Core State (F002)

1. Implementar `src/state/machine.ts` com @zazcart/toolkit
2. Criar slices: harness, sessions, logs
3. Definir schemas com createModel

### Fase 3: UI (F003)

1. Kanban board com BlueprintJS Cards
2. Feature cards com status visual
3. Dark theme integration

### Fase 4: Tauri Backend (F004-F006)

1. Rust commands para ler/escrever harness files
2. Spawn Claude CLI subprocess
3. Event streaming para logs real-time

---

## Arquivos Críticos a Criar

| Arquivo | Propósito |
|---------|-----------|
| `~/antonio/HQ/package.json` | Deps: React 18, BlueprintJS 6, toolkit |
| `~/antonio/HQ/src-tauri/Cargo.toml` | Deps Rust: serde_json, tokio |
| `~/antonio/HQ/src/state/machine.ts` | State machine principal |
| `~/antonio/HQ/.harness/features.json` | Features iniciais |
| `~/antonio/HQ/CLAUDE.md` | Instruções do projeto |
| `~/antonio/HQ/.claude/hooks/*` | Hooks adaptados |

---

## Próximos Passos Após Aprovação

1. Criar estrutura de diretórios
2. Iniciar projeto Tauri com template React
3. Instalar dependências (BlueprintJS 6, toolkit)
4. Copiar e adaptar hooks
5. Criar .harness/ com features iniciais
6. Iniciar harness (primeira sessão documenta setup)

# HQ - Harness Orchestrator & Dashboard

## Visão Geral

**HQ** é uma aplicação desktop nativa **global** (Tauri + React + BlueprintJS v6) que substitui o terminal para gerenciamento de desenvolvimento com AI agents. Funciona como o Claude Code - pode ser executado em qualquer pasta para gerenciar harnesses.

### O que é um Harness?

> **Problema:** Cada nova sessão de AI começa com ZERO memória do que aconteceu antes.
> **Solução:** Arquivos de estado (`progress.txt`, `features.json`) persistem contexto entre sessões.

**Fonte:** [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents) - Anthropic Engineering, Nov 2025

---

## Stack Tecnológico

| Camada | Tecnologia | Versão | Justificativa |
|--------|-----------|--------|---------------|
| **Runtime** | Tauri | 2.x | App nativo, leve, Rust backend |
| **Frontend** | React | 18.3.x | BlueprintJS 6 requer React 18 |
| **UI** | BlueprintJS | 6.x | Enterprise UI, Kanban components |
| **Terminal** | xterm.js | 5.x | Terminal emulator (frontend) |
| **PTY** | portable-pty | Rust | PTY management (backend) |
| **State** | @zazcart/toolkit | local | `createMachine`, validação, helpers |
| **Build** | Vite | 5.x | Fast HMR, Tauri plugin |
| **Package** | Bun | 1.2+ | Workspace, scripts |

**Dependências críticas:**
```json
// package.json
"@zazcart/toolkit": "file:/usr/local/packages/toolkit",
"@xterm/xterm": "^5.5.0",
"@xterm/addon-fit": "^0.10.0"
```

```toml
# Cargo.toml
portable-pty = "0.8"
```

---

## Estrutura do Projeto

```
~/antonio/HQ/
├── .claude/
│   ├── hooks/                    # Hooks reutilizados do MMS
│   │   ├── session-start.sh      # Context injection
│   │   ├── after-write.sh        # Contextual warnings

│   │   └── protect-package-json.sh
│   ├── settings.json             # Hook configuration
│   └── settings.local.json       # Permissions
│
├── .harness/
│   ├── progress.txt              # Session history
│   └── features.json             # Features com status
│
├── src-tauri/                    # Rust backend
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── main.rs               # Entry point
│   │   ├── commands/             # Tauri commands
│   │   │   ├── harness.rs        # Read/write harness files
│   │   │   ├── claude.rs         # Spawn Claude sessions
│   │   │   └── logs.rs           # Real-time log streaming
│   │   └── lib.rs
│   ├── Cargo.toml

│
├── src/                          # React frontend
│   ├── main.tsx                  # Entry point

│   ├── state/                    # @zazcart/toolkit machine
│   │   ├── machine.ts            # Main state machine
│   │   ├── slices/

│   │   │   ├── sessions.ts       # Active Claude sessions
│   │   │   └── logs.ts           # Real-time logs
│   │   └── index.ts
│   ├── components/

│   │   ├── SessionPanel.tsx      # Active sessions list

---

## Features (MVP)

### F001: Project Scaffolding
- Tauri 2.x + React 18 + BlueprintJS 6 + Vite
- @zazcart/toolkit integration
- xterm.js + portable-pty setup
- Basic window with dark theme

### F002: Harness State Machine
```typescript
import { createMachine, createSlice, createAsyncSlice } from '@zazcart/toolkit';

const harnessSlice = createAsyncSlice({
  id: 'harness',
  cache: { policy: 'session' },
  context: { tauriInvoke: {} as typeof invoke },
  async load(projectPath: string, { tauriInvoke }) {
    const features = await tauriInvoke('read_features', { path: projectPath });
    const progress = await tauriInvoke('read_progress', { path: projectPath });
    return { features, progress };
  },
});

const hqMachine = createMachine({
  id: 'hq',
  slices: { harness: harnessSlice, sessions: sessionsSlice, terminal: terminalSlice },
  context: { userID: null },
  trackMetadata: false, // Performance for terminal output
});
```

### F003: Kanban Board UI
**6 colunas:** `BACKLOG` | `TODO` | `DOING` | `REVIEW` | `QA` | `DONE`

- BlueprintJS Card components
- Drag-and-drop (atualiza status em features.json)
- Color coding por categoria (core, ui, polish)
- Badge com contagem por coluna
- Filter por categoria/prioridade

### F004: Terminal Integrado (xterm.js + portable-pty)
```rust
// src-tauri/src/terminal.rs
use portable_pty::{CommandBuilder, PtySize, native_pty_system};

#[tauri::command]
async fn spawn_pty(cwd: String, window: Window) -> Result<u32, String> {
    let pty_system = native_pty_system();
    let pair = pty_system.openpty(PtySize { rows: 24, cols: 80, .. })?;

    let cmd = CommandBuilder::new("claude");
    let child = pair.slave.spawn_command(cmd)?;

    // Stream output via Tauri events
    spawn(async move {
        let mut reader = pair.master.try_clone_reader()?;
        loop {
            let mut buf = [0u8; 1024];
            let n = reader.read(&mut buf)?;
            window.emit("pty-output", &buf[..n])?;
        }
    });

    Ok(child.process_id())
}

#[tauri::command]
fn write_pty(session_id: u32, data: Vec<u8>) -> Result<(), String> {
    // Write input to PTY
}

#[tauri::command]
fn resize_pty(session_id: u32, rows: u16, cols: u16) -> Result<(), String> {
    // Resize PTY
}
```

```typescript
// src/components/Terminal.tsx
import { Terminal } from '@xterm/xterm';
import { FitAddon } from '@xterm/addon-fit';
import { listen, invoke } from '@tauri-apps/api';

function TerminalView({ sessionId }: { sessionId: string }) {
  const termRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);

  useEffect(() => {
    const term = new Terminal({ theme: { background: '#1e1e1e' } });
    const fit = new FitAddon();
    term.loadAddon(fit);
    term.open(termRef.current!);
    fit.fit();

    // Receive PTY output
    const unlisten = listen('pty-output', (e) => {
      term.write(new Uint8Array(e.payload as number[]));
    });

    // Send input to PTY
    term.onData((data) => invoke('write_pty', { sessionId, data }));

    return () => { unlisten.then(f => f()); term.dispose(); };
  }, [sessionId]);

  return <div ref={termRef} style={{ height: '100%' }} />;
}
```

### F005: Project Selector (Global Mode)
- Open folder dialog (qualquer pasta do sistema)
- Detecta se pasta tem `.harness/`
- Opção de inicializar novo harness
- Recent projects list (localStorage)

### F006: Session Management
- Start Claude session em feature selecionada
- Terminal interativo full-featured
- Kill session button
- Múltiplas sessões em tabs

---

## Schemas (@zazcart/toolkit)

```typescript
import { createModel, InferType } from '@zazcart/toolkit';

export const FeatureSchema = createModel({
  id: 'string',
  category: 'string',
  priority: 'number',
  description: 'string',
  passes: 'boolean',
  blockedBy: 'string?',
  steps: 'string[]?',
  notes: 'string?',
});

export type Feature = InferType<typeof FeatureSchema>;

export const SessionSchema = createModel({
  id: 'string',
  featureId: 'string',
  pid: 'number',
  status: { enum: ['running', 'stopped', 'completed', 'failed'] as const },
  startedAt: 'date',
  logs: 'string[]',
});

export type Session = InferType<typeof SessionSchema>;
```

---

## Hooks Reutilizados do MMS

| Hook | Reusar? | Adaptação |
|------|---------|-----------|
| `session-start.sh` | ✅ | Remover refs específicas do MMS |
| `after-write.sh` | ✅ | Generalizar avisos |
| `end-of-turn-check.sh` | ✅ | Remover typecheck específico |
| `protect-package-json.sh` | ✅ | Sem alteração |
| `task-type-detector.py` | ❌ | Muito específico ao MMS |

---

## Plano de Implementação

### Fase 1: Scaffolding (F001)

1. **Criar diretório e iniciar projeto:**
   ```bash
   mkdir -p ~/antonio/HQ
   cd ~/antonio/HQ
   bun create tauri-app . --template react-ts
   ```

2. **Configurar BlueprintJS 6 + toolkit:**
   ```bash
   cd ~/antonio/HQ
   bun add @blueprintjs/core@6 @blueprintjs/icons@6
   bun add "@zazcart/toolkit@file:/usr/local/packages/toolkit"
   ```

3. **Copiar hooks do MMS:**
   ```bash
   cp -r /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/system/.claude ~/antonio/HQ/
   # Adaptar hooks para serem genéricos
   ```

4. **Criar .harness/ inicial:**
   ```bash
   mkdir -p ~/antonio/HQ/.harness
   # Criar features.json inicial com F001-F006
   # Criar progress.txt vazio
   ```

### Fase 2: Core State (F002)

1. Implementar `src/state/machine.ts` com @zazcart/toolkit
2. Criar slices: harness, sessions, logs
3. Definir schemas com createModel

### Fase 3: UI (F003)

1. Kanban board com BlueprintJS Cards
2. Feature cards com status visual
3. Dark theme integration

### Fase 4: Tauri Backend (F004-F006)

1. Rust commands para ler/escrever harness files
2. Spawn Claude CLI subprocess
3. Event streaming para logs real-time

---

## Arquivos Críticos a Criar

| Arquivo | Propósito |
|---------|-----------|
| `~/antonio/HQ/package.json` | Deps: React 18, BlueprintJS 6, toolkit |
| `~/antonio/HQ/src-tauri/Cargo.toml` | Deps Rust: serde_json, tokio |
| `~/antonio/HQ/src/state/machine.ts` | State machine principal |
| `~/antonio/HQ/.harness/features.json` | Features iniciais |
| `~/antonio/HQ/CLAUDE.md` | Instruções do projeto |
| `~/antonio/HQ/.claude/hooks/*` | Hooks adaptados |

---

## Próximos Passos Após Aprovação

1. Criar estrutura de diretórios
2. Iniciar projeto Tauri com template React
3. Instalar dependências (BlueprintJS 6, toolkit)
4. Copiar e adaptar hooks
5. Criar .harness/ com features iniciais
6. Iniciar harness (primeira sessão documenta setup)

# HQ - Harness Orchestrator & Dashboard

## Visão Geral

**HQ** é uma aplicação desktop nativa **global** (Tauri + React + BlueprintJS v6) que substitui o terminal para gerenciamento de desenvolvimento com AI agents. Funciona como o Claude Code - pode ser executado em qualquer pasta para gerenciar harnesses.

### O que é um Harness?

> **Problema:** Cada nova sessão de AI começa com ZERO memória do que aconteceu antes.
> **Solução:** Arquivos de estado (`progress.txt`, `features.json`) persistem contexto entre sessões.

**Fonte:** [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents) - Anthropic Engineering, Nov 2025

---

## Stack Tecnológico

| Camada | Tecnologia | Versão | Justificativa |
|--------|-----------|--------|---------------|
| **Runtime** | Tauri | 2.x | App nativo, leve, Rust backend |
| **Frontend** | React | 18.3.x | BlueprintJS 6 requer React 18 |
| **UI** | BlueprintJS | 6.x | Enterprise UI, Kanban components |
| **Terminal** | xterm.js | 5.x | Terminal emulator (frontend) |
| **PTY** | portable-pty | Rust | PTY management (backend) |
| **State** | @zazcart/toolkit | local | `createMachine`, validação, helpers |
| **Build** | Vite | 5.x | Fast HMR, Tauri plugin |
| **Package** | Bun | 1.2+ | Workspace, scripts |

**Dependências críticas:**
```json
// package.json
"@zazcart/toolkit": "file:/usr/local/packages/toolkit",
"@xterm/xterm": "^5.5.0",
"@xterm/addon-fit": "^0.10.0"
```

```toml
# Cargo.toml
portable-pty = "0.8"
```

---

## Estrutura do Projeto

## Features (MVP)

### F001: Project Scaffolding
- Tauri 2.x + React 18 + BlueprintJS 6 + Vite
- @zazcart/toolkit integration
- xterm.js + portable-pty setup
- Basic window with dark theme

### F002: Harness State Machine
```typescript
import { createMachine, createSlice, createAsyncSlice } from '@zazcart/toolkit';

const harnessSlice = createAsyncSlice({
  id: 'harness',
  cache: { policy: 'session' },
  context: { tauriInvoke: {} as typeof invoke },
  async load(projectPath: string, { tauriInvoke }) {
    const features = await tauriInvoke('read_features', { path: projectPath });
    const progress = await tauriInvoke('read_progress', { path: projectPath });
    return { features, progress };
  },
});

const hqMachine = createMachine({
  id: 'hq',
  slices: { harness: harnessSlice, sessions: sessionsSlice, terminal: terminalSlice },
  context: { userID: null },
  trackMetadata: false, // Performance for terminal output
});
```

### F003: Kanban Board UI
**6 colunas:** `BACKLOG` | `TODO` | `DOING` | `REVIEW` | `QA` | `DONE`

- BlueprintJS Card components
- Drag-and-drop (atualiza status em features.json)
- Color coding por categoria (core, ui, polish)
- Badge com contagem por coluna
- Filter por categoria/prioridade

### F004: Terminal Integrado (xterm.js + portable-pty)
```rust
// src-tauri/src/terminal.rs
use portable_pty::{CommandBuilder, PtySize, native_pty_system};

#[tauri::command]
async fn spawn_pty(cwd: String, window: Window) -> Result<u32, String> {
    let pty_system = native_pty_system();
    let pair = pty_system.openpty(PtySize { rows: 24, cols: 80, .. })?;

    let cmd = CommandBuilder::new("claude");
    let child = pair.slave.spawn_command(cmd)?;

    // Stream output via Tauri events
    spawn(async move {
        let mut reader = pair.master.try_clone_reader()?;
        loop {
            let mut buf = [0u8; 1024];
            let n = reader.read(&mut buf)?;
            window.emit("pty-output", &buf[..n])?;
        }
    });

    Ok(child.process_id())
}

#[tauri::command]
fn write_pty(session_id: u32, data: Vec<u8>) -> Result<(), String> {
    // Write input to PTY
}

#[tauri::command]
fn resize_pty(session_id: u32, rows: u16, cols: u16) -> Result<(), String> {
    // Resize PTY
}
```

```typescript
// src/components/Terminal.tsx
import { Terminal } from '@xterm/xterm';
import { FitAddon } from '@xterm/addon-fit';
import { listen, invoke } from '@tauri-apps/api';

function TerminalView({ sessionId }: { sessionId: string }) {
  const termRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);

  useEffect(() => {
    const term = new Terminal({ theme: { background: '#1e1e1e' } });
    const fit = new FitAddon();
    term.loadAddon(fit);
    term.open(termRef.current!);
    fit.fit();

    // Receive PTY output
    const unlisten = listen('pty-output', (e) => {
      term.write(new Uint8Array(e.payload as number[]));
    });

    // Send input to PTY
    term.onData((data) => invoke('write_pty', { sessionId, data }));

    return () => { unlisten.then(f => f()); term.dispose(); };
  }, [sessionId]);

  return <div ref={termRef} style={{ height: '100%' }} />;
}
```

### F005: Project Selector (Global Mode)
- Open folder dialog (qualquer pasta do sistema)
- Detecta se pasta tem `.harness/`
- Opção de inicializar novo harness
- Recent projects list (localStorage)

### F006: Session Management
- Start Claude session em feature selecionada
- Terminal interativo full-featured
- Kill session button
- Múltiplas sessões em tabs

---

## Schemas (@zazcart/toolkit)

```typescript
import { createModel, createType, InferType } from '@zazcart/toolkit';

// Status do Kanban (6 colunas)
export const FeatureStatusType = createType({
  enum: ['BACKLOG', 'TODO', 'DOING', 'REVIEW', 'QA', 'DONE'] as const
});

export type FeatureStatus = InferType<typeof FeatureStatusType>;

export const FeatureSchema = createModel({
  id: 'string',
  category: 'string',
  priority: 'number',
  description: 'string',
  status: { enum: ['BACKLOG', 'TODO', 'DOING', 'REVIEW', 'QA', 'DONE'] as const },
  blockedBy: 'string?',
  steps: 'string[]?',
  notes: 'string?',
});

export type Feature = InferType<typeof FeatureSchema>;

export const SessionSchema = createModel({
  id: 'string',
  featureId: 'string?',
  pid: 'number',
  cwd: 'string',
  status: { enum: ['running', 'stopped', 'completed', 'failed'] as const },
  startedAt: 'date',
});

export type Session = InferType<typeof SessionSchema>;

export const ProjectSchema = createModel({
  path: 'string',
  name: 'string',
  hasHarness: 'boolean',
  lastOpened: 'date?',
});

export type Project = InferType<typeof ProjectSchema>;
```

| `protect-package-json.sh` | ✅ | Sem alteração |

# HQ - Harness Orchestrator & Dashboard

## Visão Geral

**HQ** é uma aplicação desktop nativa **global** (Tauri + React + BlueprintJS v6) que substitui o terminal para gerenciamento de desenvolvimento com AI agents. Funciona como o Claude Code - pode ser executado em qualquer pasta para gerenciar harnesses.

### O que é um Harness?

> **Problema:** Cada nova sessão de AI começa com ZERO memória do que aconteceu antes.
> **Solução:** Arquivos de estado (`progress.txt`, `features.json`) persistem contexto entre sessões.

**Fonte:** [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents) - Anthropic Engineering, Nov 2025

---

## Stack Tecnológico

| Camada | Tecnologia | Versão | Justificativa |
|--------|-----------|--------|---------------|
| **Runtime** | Tauri | 2.x | App nativo, leve, Rust backend |
| **Frontend** | React | 18.3.x | BlueprintJS 6 requer React 18 |
| **UI** | BlueprintJS | 6.x | Enterprise UI, Kanban components |
| **Terminal** | xterm.js | 5.x | Terminal emulator (frontend) |
| **PTY** | portable-pty | Rust | PTY management (backend) |
| **State** | @zazcart/toolkit | local | `createMachine`, validação, helpers |
| **Build** | Vite | 5.x | Fast HMR, Tauri plugin |
| **Package** | Bun | 1.2+ | Workspace, scripts |

**Dependências críticas:**
```json
// package.json
"@zazcart/toolkit": "file:/usr/local/packages/toolkit",
"@xterm/xterm": "^5.5.0",
"@xterm/addon-fit": "^0.10.0"
```

```toml
# Cargo.toml
portable-pty = "0.8"
```

---

## Estrutura do Projeto

│   ├── hooks/                    # Hooks reutilizados do MMS
│   │   ├── session-start.sh      # Context injection

│   └── settings.local.json       # Permissions
│
├── .harness/

│   └── features.json             # Features com status (6 colunas Kanban)
│
├── src-tauri/                    # Rust backend
│   ├── src/
