input.content:
# HQ - Harness Orchestrator & Dashboard

## Vision

**HQ** (Headquarters) is a native desktop application that provides a visual dashboard for managing AI agent development sessions. It enables developers to orchestrate, monitor, and control multiple Claude Code sessions across different projects without using a terminal.

## Core Philosophy

> "A Single Pane of Glass for AI-Assisted Development"

HQ transforms the way developers interact with long-running AI agents by providing:
- **Visual Kanban boards** for feature tracking
- **Real-time logs** from all running processes
- **PTY-based process control** with full interactivity
- **Multi-project management** via tabs
- **Harness state visualization**

---

## Architecture Overview

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                         HQ Desktop App                               │
│                    (Tauri + React 18 + BlueprintJS 6)                │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                      │
│  ┌────────────────┐  ┌────────────────┐  ┌────────────────────────┐ │
│  │   Tab Bar      │  │   Kanban View   │  │   Logs Panel           │ │
│  │   (Projects)   │  │   (Features)    │  │   (Real-time PTY)      │ │
│  └────────────────┘  └────────────────┘  └────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                      │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                       State Management Layer                         │
│                  (@zazcart/toolkit state-machine)                    │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                       Rust Backend (Tauri)                           │
│                                                                      │
│  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────┐  │
│  │ PTY Controller  │  │ File Watcher    │  │ IPC Commands        │  │
│  │ (node-pty/rust) │  │ (notify crate)  │  │ (Tauri invoke)      │  │
│  └─────────────────┘  └─────────────────┘  └─────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                      │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                     External Processes                               │
│                                                                      │
│  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────┐  │
│  │ Claude Code #1  │  │ Claude Code #2  │  │ Other Processes     │  │
│  │ (Project A)     │  │ (Project B)     │  │ (servers, tests)    │  │
│  └─────────────────┘  └─────────────────┘  └─────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Technology Stack

| Layer | Technology | Version | Purpose |
|-------|------------|---------|---------|
| **Desktop Runtime** | Tauri | 2.x | Native app shell, Rust backend |
| **Frontend** | React | 18.3.1 | UI framework (BlueprintJS 6 requires React 18) |
| **UI Components** | BlueprintJS | 6.x | Enterprise UI components |
| **State Management** | @zazcart/toolkit | file:/usr/local/packages/toolkit | State machine + schema validation |
| **PTY Emulation** | portable-pty (Rust) | latest | Process spawning and control |
| **File Watching** | notify (Rust) | latest | Real-time harness file changes |
| **IPC** | Tauri Commands | - | Frontend ↔ Backend communication |

### Why These Choices?

**Tauri over Electron:**
- Smaller binary size (~10MB vs ~200MB)
- Native Rust backend for PTY control
- Better security model
- Lower memory footprint

**React 18 (not 19):**
- BlueprintJS 6.x requires React 18
- Stable concurrent features
- Wide ecosystem compatibility

**@zazcart/toolkit:**
- Already proven in production
- Immer-based immutable updates
- Schema validation with typebox
- useSyncExternalStore for React

---

## Feature Specifications

### F001: Multi-Project Tab Management

**Description:** Open and manage multiple projects simultaneously via tabs.

**UI Elements:**
- Tab bar at top (BlueprintJS Tabs)
- New tab button (+)
- Close tab button (X)
- Tab context menu (rename, close others, close all)

**Behavior:**
- Each tab represents a project directory
- Tab title shows project name (from package.json or folder name)
- Active tab is highlighted
- Tabs are reorderable via drag-and-drop

### F002: Kanban Board View

**Description:** Visual kanban board for feature tracking within a project.

**Columns:**
```
BACKLOG → TODO → DOING → REVIEW → QA → DONE
```

**Card Structure:**
- Feature ID (e.g., F001)
- Title
- Description (truncated)
- Status badge
- Priority indicator
- Click to expand details

**Data Source:**
- Reads from `.harness/features.json`
- Auto-updates when file changes (via file watcher)
- Status mapped to columns:
  - `passes: false` + not started → BACKLOG
  - `passes: false` + priority → TODO
  - `passes: false` + in_progress → DOING
  - `passes: false` + needs_review → REVIEW
  - `passes: false` + testing → QA
  - `passes: true` → DONE

### F003: Real-Time Log Panel

**Description:** Live output from running processes (Claude Code, servers, etc.)

**Features:**
- Scrollable log buffer (virtual scroll for performance)
- ANSI color support
- Timestamp prefix
- Process filter (show specific process)
- Search within logs
- Clear button
- Auto-scroll toggle

**Technical:**
- PTY streams → Tauri events → React state
- Max 10,000 lines per process (ring buffer)
- Log persistence to disk (optional)

### F004: PTY Process Controller

**Description:** Spawn, control, and interact with shell processes.

**Capabilities:**
- Start new process (e.g., `claude` command)
- Send keystrokes (including Ctrl+C, Ctrl+D)
- Resize PTY on window resize
- Kill process
- Restart process

**Rust Implementation:**
```rust
use portable_pty::{CommandBuilder, PtySize, native_pty_system};

fn spawn_process(cmd: &str, cols: u16, rows: u16) -> Result<PtyPair> {
    let pty_system = native_pty_system();
    let pair = pty_system.openpty(PtySize { rows, cols, .. })?;
    let cmd = CommandBuilder::new(cmd);
    pair.slave.spawn_command(cmd)?;
    Ok(pair)
}
```

### F005: Harness State Visualization

**Description:** Display current harness state (progress, features) visually.

**Components:**
- Progress summary card
  - Features completed / total
  - Current session info
  - Last commit
- Active feature highlight
- Session timeline (from progress.txt)

**Data Binding:**
- `.harness/progress.txt` → Session history
- `.harness/features.json` → Feature states
- `git log` → Commit history

### F006: Project Selector Dialog

**Description:** Open new project from file system.

**UI:**
- BlueprintJS Dialog
- Recent projects list
- Browse button (native file picker via Tauri)
- Validate project (check for .harness folder)

### F007: Settings Panel

**Description:** Configure HQ preferences.

**Settings:**
- Theme (light/dark/system)
- Log buffer size
- Auto-start Claude Code
- Default shell
- Font size
- PTY configuration

---

## State Schema (using @zazcart/toolkit)

```typescript
import { Type, Static } from 'typebox';
import { createStore, createSlice } from '@zazcart/toolkit';

// Project Schema
const ProjectSchema = Type.Object({
  id: Type.String(),
  path: Type.String(),
  name: Type.String(),
  harness: Type.Optional(Type.Object({
    progressFile: Type.String(),
    featuresFile: Type.String(),
  })),
});

// Feature Schema (from harness)
const FeatureSchema = Type.Object({
  id: Type.String(),
  description: Type.String(),
  category: Type.String(),
  passes: Type.Boolean(),
  steps: Type.Array(Type.String()),
});

// Process Schema
const ProcessSchema = Type.Object({
  id: Type.String(),
  projectId: Type.String(),
  command: Type.String(),
  status: Type.Union([
    Type.Literal('running'),
    Type.Literal('stopped'),
    Type.Literal('error'),
  ]),
  pid: Type.Optional(Type.Number()),
});

// Log Entry Schema
const LogEntrySchema = Type.Object({
  processId: Type.String(),
  timestamp: Type.Number(),
  content: Type.String(),
  level: Type.Union([
    Type.Literal('stdout'),
    Type.Literal('stderr'),
  ]),
});

// App State
const AppStateSchema = Type.Object({
  // UI State
  activeTab: Type.String(),
  theme: Type.Union([
    Type.Literal('light'),
    Type.Literal('dark'),
    Type.Literal('system'),
  ]),

  // Projects
  projects: Type.Record(Type.String(), ProjectSchema),
  projectOrder: Type.Array(Type.String()),

  // Features (per project)
  features: Type.Record(Type.String(), Type.Array(FeatureSchema)),

  // Processes
  processes: Type.Record(Type.String(), ProcessSchema),

  // Logs (ring buffer per process)
  logs: Type.Record(Type.String(), Type.Array(LogEntrySchema)),
});

type AppState = Static<typeof AppStateSchema>;
```

---

## Tauri Commands (IPC)

```typescript
// Frontend → Backend commands

interface TauriCommands {
  // Project management
  'project:open': (path: string) => Promise<Project>;
  'project:close': (id: string) => Promise<void>;
  'project:list_recent': () => Promise<string[]>;

  // Process management
  'pty:spawn': (projectId: string, cmd: string) => Promise<string>;
  'pty:write': (processId: string, data: string) => Promise<void>;
  'pty:resize': (processId: string, cols: number, rows: number) => Promise<void>;
  'pty:kill': (processId: string) => Promise<void>;

  // Harness operations
  'harness:read_features': (path: string) => Promise<Feature[]>;
  'harness:read_progress': (path: string) => Promise<string>;
  'harness:watch': (projectPath: string) => Promise<void>;
  'harness:unwatch': (projectPath: string) => Promise<void>;

  // File operations
  'fs:select_directory': () => Promise<string | null>;
  'fs:read_file': (path: string) => Promise<string>;
}

// Backend → Frontend events
interface TauriEvents {
  'pty:output': { processId: string; data: string };
  'pty:exit': { processId: string; code: number };
  'harness:changed': { projectPath: string; file: string };
}
```

---

## Directory Structure

```
~/antonio/HQ/
├── .claude/
│   ├── settings.json          # Claude hooks config
│   ├── settings.local.json    # Local permissions
│   └── hooks/
│       ├── session-start.sh   # Harness context injection
│       ├── protect-deps.sh    # Block manual package edits
│       └── quality-check.sh   # End-of-turn validation
│
├── .harness/
│   ├── progress.txt           # Session history log
│   └── features.json          # Feature requirements
│
├── docs/
│   ├── HQ_SPEC.md             # This document
│   └── ARCHITECTURE.md        # Technical deep-dive
│
├── src/                       # React frontend
│   ├── main.tsx               # Entry point
│   ├── App.tsx                # Main component
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── TabBar.tsx
│   │   ├── KanbanBoard.tsx
│   │   ├── LogPanel.tsx
│   │   ├── ProcessController.tsx
│   │   └── ProjectSelector.tsx
│   ├── state/
│   │   ├── store.ts           # @zazcart/toolkit store
│   │   ├── slices/
│   │   │   ├── projects.ts
│   │   │   ├── features.ts
│   │   │   ├── processes.ts
│   │   │   └── logs.ts
│   │   └── actions.ts
│   ├── lib/
│   │   ├── tauri.ts           # Tauri command wrappers
│   │   └── pty.ts             # PTY helpers
│   └── views/
│       ├── Dashboard.tsx
│       └── Settings.tsx
│
├── src-tauri/                 # Rust backend
│   ├── Cargo.toml
│   ├── tauri.conf.json
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── main.rs            # Entry point
│   │   ├── commands/
│   │   │   ├── project.rs
│   │   │   ├── pty.rs
│   │   │   ├── harness.rs
│   │   │   └── fs.rs
│   │   └── lib.rs
│   └── icons/
│
├── package.json
├── vite.config.ts
├── tsconfig.json
├── CLAUDE.md                  # Project rules for Claude Code
└── README.md
```

---

## Development Workflow

### Initial Setup

```bash
# 1. Create project with Tauri CLI
cd ~/antonio/HQ
bun create tauri-app --template react-ts

# 2. Add dependencies
bun add react@18 react-dom@18 @blueprintjs/core@6 @blueprintjs/icons@6
bun add "@zazcart/toolkit@file:/usr/local/packages/toolkit"

# 3. Add Rust dependencies (Cargo.toml)
# portable-pty, notify, serde, etc.

# 4. Run development
bun tauri dev
```

### Harness Protocol

1. **Read** `.harness/progress.txt` for session context
2. **Read** `.harness/features.json` for next feature
3. **Implement** one feature at a time
4. **Test** visually with the app running
5. **Commit** after feature passes
6. **Update** `.harness/progress.txt`

---

## Kanban Column Mapping

| Harness State | Kanban Column |
|---------------|---------------|
| `passes: false`, no markers | BACKLOG |
| `passes: false`, `priority: high` | TODO |
| `passes: false`, `status: in_progress` | DOING |
| `passes: false`, `status: review` | REVIEW |
| `passes: false`, `status: testing` | QA |
| `passes: true` | DONE |

**Extended features.json schema:**

```json
{
  "id": "F001",
  "description": "Multi-project tab management",
  "category": "ui",
  "passes": false,
  "priority": "high",
  "status": "in_progress",
  "steps": [
    "Create TabBar component",
    "Implement tab state management",
    "Add new/close tab actions"
  ]
}
```

---

## Performance Considerations

### Log Buffer

- Use ring buffer (fixed size array) per process
- Virtual scroll for rendering (react-window or similar)
- Debounce updates (batch 100ms)

### File Watching

- Use Rust's notify crate (efficient fs events)
- Debounce file change events (500ms)
- Only watch .harness/ directory

### PTY Streaming

- Stream data via Tauri events (not polling)
- Chunk large outputs
- Rate limit to 60fps max

---

## Security Considerations

- **Path Validation:** Validate all project paths
- **Command Sanitization:** Sanitize shell commands
- **No Remote Execution:** No network-based command execution
- **Sandboxed PTY:** Each PTY in isolated process

---

## References

- [Anthropic: Effective Harnesses for Long-Running Agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
- [Anthropic: Building Agents with Claude Agent SDK](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/building-agents-with-the-claude-agent-sdk)
- [Tauri 2.0 Documentation](https://v2.tauri.app/)
- [BlueprintJS 6.x](https://blueprintjs.com/docs/)
- [portable-pty (Rust)](https://docs.rs/portable-pty)

---

## Success Metrics

| Metric | Target |
|--------|--------|
| App startup time | < 2s |
| Log rendering latency | < 16ms (60fps) |
| PTY input latency | < 50ms |
| Memory usage (10 projects) | < 200MB |
| Binary size | < 30MB |

  content: Type.String(),
  level: Type.Union([
    Type.Literal('stdout'),
    Type.Literal('stderr'),
  ]),
});

// App State
const AppStateSchema = Type.Object({
  // UI State
  activeTab: Type.String(),
  theme: Type.Union([
    Type.Literal('light'),
    Type.Literal('dark'),
    Type.Literal('system'),
  ]),

  // Projects
  projects: Type.Record(Type.String(), ProjectSchema),
  projectOrder: Type.Array(Type.String()),

  // Features (per project)
  features: Type.Record(Type.String(), Type.Array(FeatureSchema)),

  // Processes
  processes: Type.Record(Type.String(), ProcessSchema),

  // Logs (ring buffer per process)
  logs: Type.Record(Type.String(), Type.Array(LogEntrySchema)),
});

type AppState = Static<typeof AppStateSchema>;
```

---

## Tauri Commands (IPC)

```typescript
// Frontend → Backend commands

interface TauriCommands {
  // Project management
  'project:open': (path: string) => Promise<Project>;
  'project:close': (id: string) => Promise<void>;
  'project:list_recent': () => Promise<string[]>;

  // Process management
  'pty:spawn': (projectId: string, cmd: string) => Promise<string>;
  'pty:write': (processId: string, data: string) => Promise<void>;
  'pty:resize': (processId: string, cols: number, rows: number) => Promise<void>;
  'pty:kill': (processId: string) => Promise<void>;

  // Harness operations
  'harness:read_features': (path: string) => Promise<Feature[]>;
  'harness:read_progress': (path: string) => Promise<string>;
  'harness:watch': (projectPath: string) => Promise<void>;
  'harness:unwatch': (projectPath: string) => Promise<void>;

  // File operations
  'fs:select_directory': () => Promise<string | null>;
  'fs:read_file': (path: string) => Promise<string>;
}

// Backend → Frontend events
interface TauriEvents {
  'pty:output': { processId: string; data: string };
  'pty:exit': { processId: string; code: number };
  'harness:changed': { projectPath: string; file: string };
}
```

---

## Directory Structure

```
~/antonio/HQ/
├── .claude/
│   ├── settings.json          # Claude hooks config
│   ├── settings.local.json    # Local permissions
│   └── hooks/
│       ├── session-start.sh   # Harness context injection
│       ├── protect-deps.sh    # Block manual package edits
│       └── quality-check.sh   # End-of-turn validation
│
├── .harness/
│   ├── progress.txt           # Session history log
│   └── features.json          # Feature requirements
│
├── docs/
│   ├── HQ_SPEC.md             # This document
│   └── ARCHITECTURE.md        # Technical deep-dive
│
├── src/                       # React frontend
│   ├── main.tsx               # Entry point
│   ├── App.tsx                # Main component
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── TabBar.tsx
│   │   ├── KanbanBoard.tsx
│   │   ├── LogPanel.tsx
│   │   ├── ProcessController.tsx
│   │   └── ProjectSelector.tsx
│   ├── state/
│   │   ├── store.ts           # @zazcart/toolkit store
│   │   ├── slices/
│   │   │   ├── projects.ts
│   │   │   ├── features.ts
│   │   │   ├── processes.ts
│   │   │   └── logs.ts
│   │   └── actions.ts
│   ├── lib/
│   │   ├── tauri.ts           # Tauri command wrappers
│   │   └── pty.ts             # PTY helpers
│   └── views/
│       ├── Dashboard.tsx
│       └── Settings.tsx
│
├── src-tauri/                 # Rust backend
│   ├── Cargo.toml
│   ├── tauri.conf.json
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── main.rs            # Entry point
│   │   ├── commands/
│   │   │   ├── project.rs
│   │   │   ├── pty.rs
│   │   │   ├── harness.rs
│   │   │   └── fs.rs
│   │   └── lib.rs
│   └── icons/
│
├── package.json
├── vite.config.ts
├── tsconfig.json
├── CLAUDE.md                  # Project rules for Claude Code
└── README.md
```

---

## Development Workflow

### Initial Setup

```bash
# 1. Create project with Tauri CLI
cd ~/antonio/HQ
bun create tauri-app --template react-ts

# 2. Add dependencies
bun add react@18 react-dom@18 @blueprintjs/core@6 @blueprintjs/icons@6
bun add "@zazcart/toolkit@file:/usr/local/packages/toolkit"

# 3. Add Rust dependencies (Cargo.toml)
# portable-pty, notify, serde, etc.

# 4. Run development
bun tauri dev
```

### Harness Protocol

1. **Read** `.harness/progress.txt` for session context
2. **Read** `.harness/features.json` for next feature
3. **Implement** one feature at a time
4. **Test** visually with the app running
5. **Commit** after feature passes
6. **Update** `.harness/progress.txt`

---

## Kanban Column Mapping

| Harness State | Kanban Column |
|---------------|---------------|
| `passes: false`, no markers | BACKLOG |
| `passes: false`, `priority: high` | TODO |
| `passes: false`, `status: in_progress` | DOING |
| `passes: false`, `status: review` | REVIEW |
| `passes: false`, `status: testing` | QA |
| `passes: true` | DONE |

**Extended features.json schema:**

```json
{
  "id": "F001",
  "description": "Multi-project tab management",
  "category": "ui",
  "passes": false,
  "priority": "high",
  "status": "in_progress",
  "steps": [
    "Create TabBar component",
    "Implement tab state management",
    "Add new/close tab actions"
  ]
}
```

---

## Performance Considerations

### Log Buffer

- Use ring buffer (fixed size array) per process
- Virtual scroll for rendering (react-window or similar)
- Debounce updates (batch 100ms)

### File Watching

- Use Rust's notify crate (efficient fs events)
- Debounce file change events (500ms)
- Only watch .harness/ directory

### PTY Streaming

- Stream data via Tauri events (not polling)
- Chunk large outputs
- Rate limit to 60fps max

---

## Security Considerations

- **Path Validation:** Validate all project paths
- **Command Sanitization:** Sanitize shell commands
- **No Remote Execution:** No network-based command execution
- **Sandboxed PTY:** Each PTY in isolated process

---

## References

- [Anthropic: Effective Harnesses for Long-Running Agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
- [Anthropic: Building Agents with Claude Agent SDK](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/building-agents-with-the-claude-agent-sdk)
- [Tauri 2.0 Documentation](https://v2.tauri.app/)
- [BlueprintJS 6.x](https://blueprintjs.com/docs/)
- [portable-pty (Rust)](https://docs.rs/portable-pty)

---

## Success Metrics

| Metric | Target |
|--------|--------|
| App startup time | < 2s |
| Log rendering latency | < 16ms (60fps) |
| PTY input latency | < 50ms |
| Memory usage (10 projects) | < 200MB |
| Binary size | < 30MB |

input.content:
# HQ - Harness Orchestrator & Dashboard

## Project Overview

HQ is a native desktop application (Tauri + React 18 + BlueprintJS 6) for managing AI agent development sessions. It provides visual Kanban boards, real-time logs, and PTY-based process control for multiple projects.

---

## HARNESS: Fundamental Concept

> **This project uses a Harness.** If you don't know what that is:
> - [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)

### What is a Harness?

A **Harness** is a framework that enables AI agents to work on long tasks across multiple context windows/sessions.

**Problem:** Each new Claude Code session starts with NO memory of previous sessions.

**Solution:** State files persist context between sessions:
- `.harness/progress.txt` → session history
- `.harness/features.json` → requirements with status (passes: true/false)

### Work Protocol

```
1. READ .harness/progress.txt (understand current state)
2. READ .harness/features.json (find next feature with passes: false)
3. IMPLEMENT one feature
4. TEST visually (run the app)
5. COMMIT after passing
6. UPDATE .harness/progress.txt
7. STOP (leave codebase production-ready)
```

---

## Tech Stack

| Technology | Version | Purpose |
|------------|---------|---------|
| **Tauri** | 2.x | Native desktop shell, Rust backend |
| **React** | 18.3.1 | UI framework (required for BlueprintJS 6) |
| **BlueprintJS** | 6.x | Enterprise UI components |
| **@zazcart/toolkit** | file:/usr/local/packages/toolkit | State machine + schema validation |
| **portable-pty** | latest (Rust) | PTY emulation for process control |
| **notify** | latest (Rust) | File system watching |

### Critical Version Notes

- **React 18 ONLY** - BlueprintJS 6 is incompatible with React 19
- **Tauri 2.x** - Use v2 APIs, not v1

---

## Project Structure

```
~/antonio/HQ/
├── .claude/              # Claude Code configuration
│   ├── settings.json     # Hooks configuration
│   └── hooks/            # Hook scripts
├── .harness/             # Harness state files
│   ├── progress.txt      # Session history
│   └── features.json     # Feature requirements
├── docs/
│   └── HQ_SPEC.md        # Full specification
├── src/                  # React frontend
│   ├── components/       # UI components
│   ├── state/            # @zazcart/toolkit store
│   ├── lib/              # Utilities
│   └── views/            # Page views
├── src-tauri/            # Rust backend
│   └── src/              # Tauri commands
├── package.json
├── vite.config.ts
└── CLAUDE.md             # This file
```

---

## State Management

**CRITICAL:** Use `@zazcart/toolkit` for ALL state management.

```typescript
// ✅ CORRECT: Use @zazcart/toolkit
import { createStore, useSelector } from '@zazcart/toolkit';

// ❌ WRONG: React hooks for app state
const [projects, setProjects] = useState([]); // NO
```

The toolkit provides:
- `createStore` - Immer-based state machine
- `useSelector` - React integration (useSyncExternalStore)
- `Type` from typebox - Schema validation

---

## Key Features (Kanban Columns)

```
BACKLOG → TODO → DOING → REVIEW → QA → DONE
```

Features are tracked in `.harness/features.json` with `passes: boolean`.

See `docs/HQ_SPEC.md` for complete feature list.

---

## Commands

```bash
# Development
bun tauri dev

# Build
bun tauri build

# Frontend only (for UI development)
bun run dev

# Type check
bun run typecheck
```

---

## CRITICAL AI RULES

### Rule 1: CHECK THE CURRENT DATE

```bash
date  # Verify current date
```

Your training data is outdated. Always search with current year.

### Rule 2: INSTALL DEPENDENCIES VIA COMMANDS

```bash
# CORRECT
bun add react@18 @blueprintjs/core@6

# WRONG - DO NOT manually edit package.json
```

### Rule 3: VISUAL VERIFICATION

You cannot see the app. Test visually:
1. Run `bun tauri dev`
2. Take screenshots with Playwright MCP
3. Verify UI renders correctly

### Rule 4: STOP-SEARCH-UNDERSTAND-APPLY

When stuck (2+ failed attempts):
1. STOP - Don't guess
2. SEARCH - WebSearch with current year
3. UNDERSTAND - Read documentation
4. APPLY - Use correct solution

---

## Documentation

- `docs/HQ_SPEC.md` - Full system specification
- `.harness/progress.txt` - Session history
- `.harness/features.json` - Feature requirements

---

## Quick Reference

### Tauri Commands (Rust → TypeScript)

```typescript
import { invoke } from '@tauri-apps/api/core';

// Invoke Rust command
const result = await invoke('pty_spawn', { cmd: 'claude' });
```

### BlueprintJS Components

```tsx
import { Button, Card, Tabs, Tag } from '@blueprintjs/core';
import '@blueprintjs/core/lib/css/blueprint.css';
```

### @zazcart/toolkit State

```typescript
import { createStore, Type } from '@zazcart/toolkit';

const store = createStore({
  schema: Type.Object({ ... }),
  initial: { ... },
});
```

#!/bin/bash
# Hook: SessionStart - Harness Context Injection
# Purpose: Ensure Claude understands harness concept at session start
# Always exit 0 (informational only)

PROJECT_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$(pwd)}"
cd "$PROJECT_DIR" 2>/dev/null || exit 0

echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
echo "🏢 HQ HARNESS - SESSION START"
echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"

# WHAT IS A HARNESS?
echo ""
echo "┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐"
echo "│ WHAT IS A HARNESS?                                          │"
echo "├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤"
echo "│ A 'Harness' is a framework that enables AI agents to work   │"
echo "│ on long tasks across multiple context windows/sessions.     │"
echo "│                                                              │"
echo "│ PROBLEM: Each new session starts with NO memory.            │"
echo "│ SOLUTION: State files (progress + features) persist context.│"
echo "│                                                              │"
echo "│ Official source: anthropic.com/engineering/                 │"
echo "│                  effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents│"
echo "└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘"

# Current date
echo ""
echo "📅 Current date: $(date '+%Y-%m-%d')"

# Work protocol
echo ""
echo "┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐"
echo "│ WORK PROTOCOL                                               │"
echo "├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤"
echo "│ 1. READ .harness/progress.txt → session history            │"
echo "│ 2. READ .harness/features.json → next feature (passes:false)│"
echo "│ 3. IMPLEMENT one feature at a time                          │"
echo "│ 4. TEST visually (bun tauri dev)                            │"
echo "│ 5. COMMIT after feature passes                              │"
echo "│ 6. UPDATE .harness/progress.txt                             │"
echo "└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘"

# Project info
echo ""
echo "📦 HQ - Harness Orchestrator & Dashboard"
echo "   Stack: Tauri 2.x + React 18 + BlueprintJS 6"
echo "   State: @zazcart/toolkit (file:/usr/local/packages/toolkit)"

# Package versions
if [ -f "package.json" ]; then
  echo ""
  echo "📦 Current package versions:"
  jq -r '.dependencies | to_entries[] | select(.key | test("react|blueprint|tauri")) | "   \(.key): \(.value)"' package.json 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Recent git activity
if git rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo ""
  echo "📜 Recent commits:"
  git --no-pager log --oneline -3 2>/dev/null | sed 's/^/   /'
fi

# Harness status
echo ""
if [ -f ".harness/features.json" ]; then
  TOTAL=$(jq 'length' .harness/features.json 2>/dev/null || echo "?")
  DONE=$(jq '[.[] | select(.passes == true)] | length' .harness/features.json 2>/dev/null || echo "?")
  echo "📊 Features: $DONE/$TOTAL complete"
else
  echo "⚠️  .harness/features.json not found"
fi

exit 0

  jq -r '.dependencies | to_entries[] | select(.key | test("react|blueprint|tauri")) | "   \(.key): \(.value)"' package.json 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Recent git activity
if git rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo ""
  echo "📜 Recent commits:"
  git --no-pager log --oneline -3 2>/dev/null | sed 's/^/   /'
fi

# Harness status
echo ""
if [ -f ".harness/features.json" ]; then
  TOTAL=$(jq 'length' .harness/features.json 2>/dev/null || echo "?")
  DONE=$(jq '[.[] | select(.passes == true)] | length' .harness/features.json 2>/dev/null || echo "?")
  echo "📊 Features: $DONE/$TOTAL complete"
else
  echo "⚠️  .harness/features.json not found"
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
# Hook: PreToolUse (Write|Edit)
# Purpose: BLOCK manual edits to dependencies in package.json or Cargo.toml
# Exit 2 = BLOCK, Exit 0 = ALLOW

FILE_PATH=$(jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty' 2>/dev/null)
OLD_STRING=$(jq -r '.tool_input.old_string // empty' 2>/dev/null)
NEW_STRING=$(jq -r '.tool_input.new_string // empty' 2>/dev/null)
CONTENT=$(jq -r '.tool_input.content // empty' 2>/dev/null)

# Check package.json
if [[ "$FILE_PATH" == *"package.json"* ]]; then
  if [[ "$OLD_STRING" == *'"dependencies"'* ]] || \
     [[ "$OLD_STRING" == *'"devDependencies"'* ]] || \
     [[ "$NEW_STRING" == *'"dependencies"'* ]] || \
     [[ "$NEW_STRING" == *'"devDependencies"'* ]] || \
     [[ "$CONTENT" == *'"dependencies"'* ]] || \
     [[ "$CONTENT" == *'"devDependencies"'* ]]; then
    echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━" >&2
    echo "🚫 BLOCKED: Manual edit to dependencies in package.json" >&2
    echo "" >&2
    echo "Use 'bun add <package>' instead of editing manually." >&2
    echo "Package managers resolve versions and peer dependencies." >&2
    echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━" >&2
    exit 2
  fi
fi

# Check Cargo.toml
if [[ "$FILE_PATH" == *"Cargo.toml"* ]]; then
  if [[ "$OLD_STRING" == *"[dependencies]"* ]] || \
     [[ "$NEW_STRING" == *"[dependencies]"* ]] || \
     [[ "$CONTENT" == *"[dependencies]"* ]]; then
    echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━" >&2
    echo "🚫 BLOCKED: Manual edit to dependencies in Cargo.toml" >&2
    echo "" >&2
    echo "Use 'cargo add <crate>' instead of editing manually." >&2
    echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━" >&2
    exit 2
  fi
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
# Hook: Stop (Quality Gate)
# Purpose: End-of-turn validation
# Exit 2 = BLOCK continuation, Exit 0 = Allow

PROJECT_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$(pwd)}"
cd "$PROJECT_DIR" 2>/dev/null || exit 0

echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
echo "🔍 END-OF-TURN QUALITY CHECK"
echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"

# 1. TypeScript check
TS_CHANGED=false
if git diff --name-only HEAD 2>/dev/null | grep -qE '\.(ts|tsx)$'; then
  TS_CHANGED=true
fi
if git ls-files --others --exclude-standard 2>/dev/null | grep -qE '\.(ts|tsx)$'; then
  TS_CHANGED=true
fi

if [ "$TS_CHANGED" = true ]; then
  if [ -f "package.json" ] && grep -q '"typecheck"' package.json 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "📝 TypeScript files changed - running typecheck..."
    if ! bun run typecheck 2>&1 | head -20; then
      echo "" >&2
      echo "❌ TYPECHECK FAILED" >&2
      echo "Fix TypeScript errors before continuing." >&2
      exit 2
    fi
    echo "✅ Typecheck passed"
  fi
fi

# 2. Rust check
RS_CHANGED=false
if git diff --name-only HEAD 2>/dev/null | grep -qE '\.rs$'; then
  RS_CHANGED=true
fi
if git ls-files --others --exclude-standard 2>/dev/null | grep -qE '\.rs$'; then
  RS_CHANGED=true
fi

if [ "$RS_CHANGED" = true ]; then
  if [ -f "src-tauri/Cargo.toml" ]; then
    echo "🦀 Rust files changed - running cargo check..."
    if ! (cd src-tauri && cargo check 2>&1 | head -20); then
      echo "" >&2
      echo "❌ CARGO CHECK FAILED" >&2
      echo "Fix Rust errors before continuing." >&2
      exit 2
    fi
    echo "✅ Cargo check passed"
  fi
fi

# 3. Harness file validation
if [ ! -f ".harness/features.json" ]; then
  echo "⚠️  .harness/features.json not found" >&2
fi

if [ ! -f ".harness/progress.txt" ]; then
  echo "⚠️  .harness/progress.txt not found - update progress log" >&2
fi

echo ""
echo "✅ End-of-turn check complete"
exit 0

input.content:
# HQ - Harness Orchestrator & Dashboard

A native desktop application for managing AI agent development sessions.

## Stack

- **Tauri 2.x** - Native desktop shell
- **React 18** - UI framework
- **BlueprintJS 6** - Enterprise UI components
- **@zazcart/toolkit** - State management

## Development

```bash
# Install dependencies
bun install

# Run frontend only
bun run dev

# Run with Tauri (full app)
bun tauri dev

# Build for production
bun tauri build
```

## Harness

This project uses a harness for persistent context across AI sessions.

- `.harness/progress.txt` - Session history
- `.harness/features.json` - Feature requirements

See `docs/HQ_SPEC.md` for full specification.

Projeto HQ criado com sucesso! Aqui está o resumo:

---

## Projeto HQ Criado

**Localização:** `~/antonio/HQ`

### Estrutura Final

```
~/antonio/HQ/
├── .claude/
│   ├── settings.json          # Hooks config
│   ├── settings.local.json    # Permissions (local)
│   └── hooks/
│       ├── session-start.sh   # Harness context injection
│       ├── protect-deps.sh    # Block manual package edits
│       └── quality-check.sh   # End-of-turn validation
├── .harness/
│   ├── progress.txt           # Session history
│   └── features.json          # 10 features defined
├── docs/
│   └── HQ_SPEC.md             # Full specification
├── src/                       # React frontend
│   ├── main.tsx
│   ├── App.tsx
│   └── index.css
├── src-tauri/                 # Rust backend
│   ├── Cargo.toml
│   ├── src/lib.rs
│   └── tauri.conf.json
├── CLAUDE.md                  # Project rules
├── README.md
└── package.json
```

### Dependencies Installed

- **react@18.3.1** + **react-dom@18.3.1**
- **@blueprintjs/core@6.4.1** + **@blueprintjs/icons@6.3.2**
- **@zazcart/toolkit** (linked from `/usr/local/packages/toolkit`)
- **@tauri-apps/cli@2.9.6** + **@tauri-apps/api@2.9.1**
- **vite@7.3.0** + **typescript@5.9.3**

### Features Defined (in features.json)

| ID | Feature | Priority |
|----|---------|----------|
| F001 | Multi-project tab management | high |
| F002 | Kanban board view | high |
| F003 | Real-time log panel | high |
| F004 | PTY process controller | critical |
| F005 | Harness state visualization | medium |
| F006 | Project selector dialog | high |
| F007 | Settings panel | low |
| F008 | File watcher | high |
| F009 | State management setup | critical |
| F010 | Tauri project initialization | critical |

### Next Steps

```bash
cd ~/antonio/HQ

# Test frontend only
bun run dev

# Test full Tauri app
bun tauri dev
```

### Sources

- [Anthropic: Effective Harnesses for Long-Running Agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
- [Anthropic: Building Agents with Claude Agent SDK](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/building-agents-with-the-claude-agent-sdk)

2. Atualizar todos os documentos (CLAUDE.md, HQ_SPEC.md, progress.txt, features.json)

Here's what I found about PatternFly 6 and React 19:

## React 19 Support Status

For the Q2 2025 PatternFly release, they made progress in several key initiatives, including PatternFly 6 migration enablement, component animations, React 19 support, and the continued evolution of PatternFly AI.(no content)For the Q2 2025 PatternFly release, they completed their initiative to support React 19 across PatternFly and made significant progress towards finalizing component animations.(no content)PatternFly now includes support for React 19 across all components and extensions, enabling users to benefit from new React features and performance enhancements. This update is non-breaking, and PatternFly 6 will continue to support React 17 and 18 in line with their support policy.

## Key Resources

**Official Documentation:**
- Main site: https://www.patternfly.org/
- Release highlights: https://www.patternfly.org/get-started/release-highlights/
- Upgrade guide: https://www.patternfly.org/get-started/upgrade/

**React Packages:**
PatternFly's React library contains a collection of components for building interfaces with consistent markup, styling, and behavior. Key npm packages include:
- `@patternfly/react-core`: Most React core components
- `@patternfly/react-charts`: PatternFly charting components
- `@patternfly/react-code-editor`: A PatternFly wrapper for the Monaco code editor
- `@patternfly/react-icons`: PatternFly icons as React components(no content)The latest version of `@patternfly/react-core` is 6.3.0, published 14 days ago.

## Migration Resources

The React 19 migration initiative prioritizes non-breaking changes in PatternFly 6 to enable React 19.(no content)PatternFly has also published new documentation on using AI to streamline code migrations, offering strategies for using AI to make it easier and faster to migrate projects to PatternFly React from other frameworks.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Here's what I found about PatternFly 6 and React 19:

## React 19 Support Status

For the Q2 2025 PatternFly release, they made progress in several key initiatives, including PatternFly 6 migration enablement, component animations, React 19 support, and the continued evolution of PatternFly AI.(no content)For the Q2 2025 PatternFly release, they completed their initiative to support React 19 across PatternFly and made significant progress towards finalizing component animations.(no content)PatternFly now includes support for React 19 across all components and extensions, enabling users to benefit from new React features and performance enhancements. This update is non-breaking, and PatternFly 6 will continue to support React 17 and 18 in line with their support policy.

## Key Resources

**Official Documentation:**
- Main site: https://www.patternfly.org/
- Release highlights: https://www.patternfly.org/get-started/release-highlights/
- Upgrade guide: https://www.patternfly.org/get-started/upgrade/

**React Packages:**
PatternFly's React library contains a collection of components for building interfaces with consistent markup, styling, and behavior. Key npm packages include:
- `@patternfly/react-core`: Most React core components
- `@patternfly/react-charts`: PatternFly charting components
- `@patternfly/react-code-editor`: A PatternFly wrapper for the Monaco code editor
- `@patternfly/react-icons`: PatternFly icons as React components(no content)The latest version of `@patternfly/react-core` is 6.3.0, published 14 days ago.

## Migration Resources

The React 19 migration initiative prioritizes non-breaking changes in PatternFly 6 to enable React 19.(no content)PatternFly has also published new documentation on using AI to streamline code migrations, offering strategies for using AI to make it easier and faster to migrate projects to PatternFly React from other frameworks.

input.content:
import {
  Brand,
  Button,
  Card,
  CardBody,
  CardTitle,
  Label,
  Masthead,
  MastheadBrand,
  MastheadMain,
  Page,
  PageSection,
  Text,
  TextContent,
  Title,
} from '@patternfly/react-core';
import { useState } from 'react';

function App() {
  const [count, setCount] = useState(0);

  const header = (
    <Masthead>
      <MastheadMain>
        <MastheadBrand>
          <Brand src="" alt="HQ">
            <Title headingLevel="h1" size="xl">HQ</Title>
          </Brand>
        </MastheadBrand>
        <Label color="blue" isCompact>Harness Orchestrator</Label>
      </MastheadMain>
    </Masthead>
  );

  return (
    <Page header={header}>
      <PageSection>
        <Card>
          <CardTitle>Welcome to HQ</CardTitle>
          <CardBody>
            <TextContent>
              <Text>Your dashboard for managing AI agent development sessions.</Text>
              <Text>Click count: {count}</Text>
            </TextContent>
            <Button variant="primary" onClick={() => setCount((c) => c + 1)}>
              Increment
            </Button>
          </CardBody>
        </Card>
      </PageSection>

      <PageSection>
        <Card>
          <CardTitle>Kanban Board</CardTitle>
          <CardBody>
            <TextContent>
              <Text>BACKLOG | TODO | DOING | REVIEW | QA | DONE</Text>
            </TextContent>
            <Label color="gold">Coming Soon</Label>
          </CardBody>
        </Card>
      </PageSection>
    </Page>
  );
}

export default App;

input.content:
# HQ - Harness Orchestrator & Dashboard

## Vision

**HQ** (Headquarters) is a native desktop application that provides a visual dashboard for managing AI agent development sessions. It enables developers to orchestrate, monitor, and control multiple Claude Code sessions across different projects without using a terminal.

## Core Philosophy

> "A Single Pane of Glass for AI-Assisted Development"

HQ transforms the way developers interact with long-running AI agents by providing:
- **Visual Kanban boards** for feature tracking
- **Real-time logs** from all running processes
- **PTY-based process control** with full interactivity
- **Multi-project management** via tabs
- **Harness state visualization**

---

## Architecture Overview

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                         HQ Desktop App                               │
│                    (Tauri + React 19 + PatternFly 6)                 │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                      │
│  ┌────────────────┐  ┌────────────────┐  ┌────────────────────────┐ │
│  │   Tab Bar      │  │   Kanban View   │  │   Logs Panel           │ │
│  │   (Projects)   │  │   (Features)    │  │   (Real-time PTY)      │ │
│  └────────────────┘  └────────────────┘  └────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                      │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                       State Management Layer                         │
│                  (@zazcart/toolkit state-machine)                    │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                       Rust Backend (Tauri)                           │
│                                                                      │
│  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────┐  │
│  │ PTY Controller  │  │ File Watcher    │  │ IPC Commands        │  │
│  │ (portable-pty)  │  │ (notify crate)  │  │ (Tauri invoke)      │  │
│  └─────────────────┘  └─────────────────┘  └─────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                      │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                     External Processes                               │
│                                                                      │
│  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────┐  │
│  │ Claude Code #1  │  │ Claude Code #2  │  │ Other Processes     │  │
│  │ (Project A)     │  │ (Project B)     │  │ (servers, tests)    │  │
│  └─────────────────┘  └─────────────────┘  └─────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Technology Stack

| Layer | Technology | Version | Purpose |
|-------|------------|---------|---------|
| **Desktop Runtime** | Tauri | 2.x | Native app shell, Rust backend |
| **Frontend** | React | 19.x | UI framework |
| **UI Components** | PatternFly | 6.x | Enterprise UI (chat, terminal, logs) |
| **State Management** | @zazcart/toolkit | file:/usr/local/packages/toolkit | State machine + schema validation |
| **PTY Emulation** | portable-pty (Rust) | latest | Process spawning and control |
| **File Watching** | notify (Rust) | latest | Real-time harness file changes |
| **IPC** | Tauri Commands | - | Frontend ↔ Backend communication |

### Why PatternFly 6?

PatternFly provides ready-made components for our use cases:
- **Chat/AI components** - For agent communication UI
- **Log viewer** - Real-time log display with ANSI support
- **Terminal** - Embedded terminal views
- **Tabs** - Multi-project navigation
- **Cards/Panels** - Kanban board layout
- **Dark theme** - Built-in dark mode support

---

## Feature Specifications

### F001: Multi-Project Tab Management

**Description:** Open and manage multiple projects simultaneously via tabs.

**UI Elements:**
- Tab bar at top (PatternFly Tabs)
- New tab button (+)
- Close tab button (X)
- Tab context menu (rename, close others, close all)

**Behavior:**
- Each tab represents a project directory
- Tab title shows project name (from package.json or folder name)
- Active tab is highlighted
- Tabs are reorderable via drag-and-drop

### F002: Kanban Board View

**Description:** Visual kanban board for feature tracking within a project.

**Columns:**
```
BACKLOG → TODO → DOING → REVIEW → QA → DONE
```

**Card Structure:**
- Feature ID (e.g., F001)
- Title
- Description (truncated)
- Status badge
- Priority indicator
- Click to expand details

**Data Source:**
- Reads from `.harness/features.json`
- Auto-updates when file changes (via file watcher)
- Status mapped to columns:
  - `passes: false` + not started → BACKLOG
  - `passes: false` + priority → TODO
  - `passes: false` + in_progress → DOING
  - `passes: false` + needs_review → REVIEW
  - `passes: false` + testing → QA
  - `passes: true` → DONE

### F003: Real-Time Log Panel

**Description:** Live output from running processes (Claude Code, servers, etc.)

**Features:**
- PatternFly LogViewer component
- ANSI color support
- Timestamp prefix
- Process filter
- Search within logs
- Clear button
- Auto-scroll toggle

**Technical:**
- PTY streams → Tauri events → React state
- Max 10,000 lines per process (ring buffer)

### F004: PTY Process Controller

**Description:** Spawn, control, and interact with shell processes.

**Capabilities:**
- Start new process (e.g., `claude` command)
- Send keystrokes (including Ctrl+C, Ctrl+D)
- Resize PTY on window resize
- Kill process
- Restart process

**Rust Implementation:**
```rust
use portable_pty::{CommandBuilder, PtySize, native_pty_system};

fn spawn_process(cmd: &str, cols: u16, rows: u16) -> Result<PtyPair> {
    let pty_system = native_pty_system();
    let pair = pty_system.openpty(PtySize { rows, cols, .. })?;
    let cmd = CommandBuilder::new(cmd);
    pair.slave.spawn_command(cmd)?;
    Ok(pair)
}
```

### F005: Harness State Visualization

**Description:** Display current harness state visually.

**Components:**
- Progress summary card
  - Features completed / total
  - Current session info
  - Last commit
- Active feature highlight
- Session timeline (from progress.txt)

### F006: Project Selector Dialog

**Description:** Open new project from file system.

**UI:**
- PatternFly Modal
- Recent projects list
- Browse button (native file picker via Tauri)
- Validate project (check for .harness folder)

### F007: Settings Panel

**Description:** Configure HQ preferences.

**Settings:**
- Theme (light/dark/system)
- Log buffer size
- Auto-start Claude Code
- Default shell
- Font size

---

## State Schema (using @zazcart/toolkit)

```typescript
import { Type, Static } from 'typebox';
import { createStore } from '@zazcart/toolkit';

// Project Schema
const ProjectSchema = Type.Object({
  id: Type.String(),
  path: Type.String(),
  name: Type.String(),
  harness: Type.Optional(Type.Object({
    progressFile: Type.String(),
    featuresFile: Type.String(),
  })),
});

// Feature Schema (from harness)
const FeatureSchema = Type.Object({
  id: Type.String(),
  description: Type.String(),
  category: Type.String(),
  passes: Type.Boolean(),
  steps: Type.Array(Type.String()),
});

// Process Schema
const ProcessSchema = Type.Object({
  id: Type.String(),
  projectId: Type.String(),
  command: Type.String(),
  status: Type.Union([
    Type.Literal('running'),
    Type.Literal('stopped'),
    Type.Literal('error'),
  ]),
  pid: Type.Optional(Type.Number()),
});

// App State
const AppStateSchema = Type.Object({
  activeTab: Type.String(),
  theme: Type.Union([
    Type.Literal('light'),
    Type.Literal('dark'),
    Type.Literal('system'),
  ]),
  projects: Type.Record(Type.String(), ProjectSchema),
  projectOrder: Type.Array(Type.String()),
  features: Type.Record(Type.String(), Type.Array(FeatureSchema)),
  processes: Type.Record(Type.String(), ProcessSchema),
});

type AppState = Static<typeof AppStateSchema>;
```

---

## Tauri Commands (IPC)

```typescript
// Frontend → Backend commands
interface TauriCommands {
  'project:open': (path: string) => Promise<Project>;
  'project:close': (id: string) => Promise<void>;
  'pty:spawn': (projectId: string, cmd: string) => Promise<string>;
  'pty:write': (processId: string, data: string) => Promise<void>;
  'pty:resize': (processId: string, cols: number, rows: number) => Promise<void>;
  'pty:kill': (processId: string) => Promise<void>;
  'harness:read_features': (path: string) => Promise<Feature[]>;
  'harness:watch': (projectPath: string) => Promise<void>;
  'fs:select_directory': () => Promise<string | null>;
}

// Backend → Frontend events
interface TauriEvents {
  'pty:output': { processId: string; data: string };
  'pty:exit': { processId: string; code: number };
  'harness:changed': { projectPath: string; file: string };
}
```

---

## Directory Structure

```
~/antonio/HQ/
├── .claude/
│   ├── settings.json
│   └── hooks/
├── .harness/
│   ├── progress.txt
│   └── features.json
├── docs/
│   └── HQ_SPEC.md
├── src/
│   ├── main.tsx
│   ├── App.tsx
│   ├── components/
│   ├── state/
│   ├── lib/
│   └── views/
├── src-tauri/
│   ├── Cargo.toml
│   ├── tauri.conf.json
│   └── src/
├── package.json
├── vite.config.ts
├── CLAUDE.md
└── README.md
```

---

## References

- [Anthropic: Effective Harnesses for Long-Running Agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
- [PatternFly 6 Documentation](https://www.patternfly.org/)
- [Tauri 2.0 Documentation](https://v2.tauri.app/)
- [portable-pty (Rust)](https://docs.rs/portable-pty)

---

## Success Metrics

| Metric | Target |
|--------|--------|
| App startup time | < 2s |
| Log rendering latency | < 16ms (60fps) |
| PTY input latency | < 50ms |
| Memory usage (10 projects) | < 200MB |
| Binary size | < 30MB |

# HQ - Harness Orchestrator & Dashboard

## Vision

**HQ** (Headquarters) is a native desktop application that provides a visual dashboard for managing AI agent development sessions. It enables developers to orchestrate, monitor, and control multiple Claude Code sessions across different projects without using a terminal.

## Core Philosophy

> "A Single Pane of Glass for AI-Assisted Development"

HQ transforms the way developers interact with long-running AI agents by providing:
- **Visual Kanban boards** for feature tracking
- **Real-time logs** from all running processes
- **PTY-based process control** with full interactivity
- **Multi-project management** via tabs
- **Harness state visualization**

---

## Architecture Overview

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                         HQ Desktop App                               │
│                    (Tauri + React 19 + PatternFly 6)                 │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                      │
│  ┌────────────────┐  ┌────────────────┐  ┌────────────────────────┐ │
│  │   Tab Bar      │  │   Kanban View   │  │   Logs Panel           │ │
│  │   (Projects)   │  │   (Features)    │  │   (Real-time PTY)      │ │
│  └────────────────┘  └────────────────┘  └────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                      │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                       State Management Layer                         │
│                  (@zazcart/toolkit state-machine)                    │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                       Rust Backend (Tauri)                           │
│                                                                      │
│  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────┐  │
│  │ PTY Controller  │  │ File Watcher    │  │ IPC Commands        │  │
│  │ (portable-pty)  │  │ (notify crate)  │  │ (Tauri invoke)      │  │
│  └─────────────────┘  └─────────────────┘  └─────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                      │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                     External Processes                               │
│                                                                      │
│  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────┐  │
│  │ Claude Code #1  │  │ Claude Code #2  │  │ Other Processes     │  │
│  │ (Project A)     │  │ (Project B)     │  │ (servers, tests)    │  │
│  └─────────────────┘  └─────────────────┘  └─────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Technology Stack

| Layer | Technology | Version | Purpose |
|-------|------------|---------|---------|
| **Desktop Runtime** | Tauri | 2.x | Native app shell, Rust backend |
| **Frontend** | React | 19.x | UI framework |
| **UI Components** | PatternFly | 6.x | Enterprise UI (chat, terminal, logs) |
| **State Management** | @zazcart/toolkit | file:/usr/local/packages/toolkit | State machine + schema validation |
| **PTY Emulation** | portable-pty (Rust) | latest | Process spawning and control |
| **File Watching** | notify (Rust) | latest | Real-time harness file changes |
| **IPC** | Tauri Commands | - | Frontend ↔ Backend communication |

### Why PatternFly 6?

PatternFly provides ready-made components for our use cases:
- **Chat/AI components** - For agent communication UI
- **Log viewer** - Real-time log display with ANSI support
- **Terminal** - Embedded terminal views
- **Tabs** - Multi-project navigation
- **Cards/Panels** - Kanban board layout
- **Dark theme** - Built-in dark mode support

---

## Feature Specifications

### F001: Multi-Project Tab Management

**Description:** Open and manage multiple projects simultaneously via tabs.

**UI Elements:**
- Tab bar at top (PatternFly Tabs)
- New tab button (+)
- Close tab button (X)
- Tab context menu (rename, close others, close all)

**Behavior:**
- Each tab represents a project directory
- Tab title shows project name (from package.json or folder name)
- Active tab is highlighted
- Tabs are reorderable via drag-and-drop

### F002: Kanban Board View

**Description:** Visual kanban board for feature tracking within a project.

**Columns:**
```
BACKLOG → TODO → DOING → REVIEW → QA → DONE
```

**Card Structure:**
- Feature ID (e.g., F001)
- Title
- Description (truncated)
- Status badge
- Priority indicator
- Click to expand details

**Data Source:**
- Reads from `.harness/features.json`
- Auto-updates when file changes (via file watcher)
- Status mapped to columns:
  - `passes: false` + not started → BACKLOG
  - `passes: false` + priority → TODO
  - `passes: false` + in_progress → DOING
  - `passes: false` + needs_review → REVIEW
  - `passes: false` + testing → QA
  - `passes: true` → DONE

### F003: Real-Time Log Panel

**Description:** Live output from running processes (Claude Code, servers, etc.)

**Features:**
- PatternFly LogViewer component
- ANSI color support
- Timestamp prefix
- Process filter
- Search within logs
- Clear button
- Auto-scroll toggle

**Technical:**
- PTY streams → Tauri events → React state
- Max 10,000 lines per process (ring buffer)

### F004: PTY Process Controller

**Description:** Spawn, control, and interact with shell processes.

**Capabilities:**
- Start new process (e.g., `claude` command)
- Send keystrokes (including Ctrl+C, Ctrl+D)
- Resize PTY on window resize
- Kill process
- Restart process

**Rust Implementation:**
```rust
use portable_pty::{CommandBuilder, PtySize, native_pty_system};

fn spawn_process(cmd: &str, cols: u16, rows: u16) -> Result<PtyPair> {
    let pty_system = native_pty_system();
    let pair = pty_system.openpty(PtySize { rows, cols, .. })?;
    let cmd = CommandBuilder::new(cmd);
    pair.slave.spawn_command(cmd)?;
    Ok(pair)
}
```

### F005: Harness State Visualization

**Description:** Display current harness state visually.

**Components:**
- Progress summary card
  - Features completed / total
  - Current session info
  - Last commit
- Active feature highlight
- Session timeline (from progress.txt)

### F006: Project Selector Dialog

**Description:** Open new project from file system.

**UI:**
- PatternFly Modal
- Recent projects list
- Browse button (native file picker via Tauri)
- Validate project (check for .harness folder)

### F007: Settings Panel

**Description:** Configure HQ preferences.

**Settings:**
- Theme (light/dark/system)
- Log buffer size
- Auto-start Claude Code
- Default shell
- Font size

---

## State Schema (using @zazcart/toolkit)

```typescript
import { Type, Static } from 'typebox';
import { createStore } from '@zazcart/toolkit';

// Project Schema
const ProjectSchema = Type.Object({
  id: Type.String(),
  path: Type.String(),
  name: Type.String(),
  harness: Type.Optional(Type.Object({
    progressFile: Type.String(),
    featuresFile: Type.String(),
  })),
});

// Feature Schema (from harness)
const FeatureSchema = Type.Object({
  id: Type.String(),
  description: Type.String(),
  category: Type.String(),
  passes: Type.Boolean(),
  steps: Type.Array(Type.String()),
});

// Process Schema
const ProcessSchema = Type.Object({
  id: Type.String(),
  projectId: Type.String(),
  command: Type.String(),
  status: Type.Union([
    Type.Literal('running'),
    Type.Literal('stopped'),
    Type.Literal('error'),
  ]),
  pid: Type.Optional(Type.Number()),
});

// App State
const AppStateSchema = Type.Object({
  activeTab: Type.String(),
  theme: Type.Union([
    Type.Literal('light'),
    Type.Literal('dark'),
    Type.Literal('system'),
  ]),
  projects: Type.Record(Type.String(), ProjectSchema),
  projectOrder: Type.Array(Type.String()),
  features: Type.Record(Type.String(), Type.Array(FeatureSchema)),
  processes: Type.Record(Type.String(), ProcessSchema),
});

type AppState = Static<typeof AppStateSchema>;
```

---

## Tauri Commands (IPC)

```typescript
// Frontend → Backend commands
interface TauriCommands {
  'project:open': (path: string) => Promise<Project>;
  'project:close': (id: string) => Promise<void>;
  'pty:spawn': (projectId: string, cmd: string) => Promise<string>;
  'pty:write': (processId: string, data: string) => Promise<void>;
  'pty:resize': (processId: string, cols: number, rows: number) => Promise<void>;
  'pty:kill': (processId: string) => Promise<void>;
  'harness:read_features': (path: string) => Promise<Feature[]>;
  'harness:watch': (projectPath: string) => Promise<void>;
  'fs:select_directory': () => Promise<string | null>;
}

// Backend → Frontend events
interface TauriEvents {
  'pty:output': { processId: string; data: string };
  'pty:exit': { processId: string; code: number };
  'harness:changed': { projectPath: string; file: string };
}
```

---

## Directory Structure

```
~/antonio/HQ/
├── .claude/
│   ├── settings.json
│   └── hooks/
├── .harness/
│   ├── progress.txt
│   └── features.json
├── docs/
│   └── HQ_SPEC.md
├── src/
│   ├── main.tsx
│   ├── App.tsx
│   ├── components/
│   ├── state/
│   ├── lib/
│   └── views/
├── src-tauri/
│   ├── Cargo.toml
│   ├── tauri.conf.json
│   └── src/
├── package.json
├── vite.config.ts
├── CLAUDE.md
└── README.md
```

---

## References

- [Anthropic: Effective Harnesses for Long-Running Agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
- [PatternFly 6 Documentation](https://www.patternfly.org/)
- [Tauri 2.0 Documentation](https://v2.tauri.app/)
- [portable-pty (Rust)](https://docs.rs/portable-pty)

---

## Success Metrics

| Metric | Target |
|--------|--------|
| App startup time | < 2s |
| Log rendering latency | < 16ms (60fps) |
| PTY input latency | < 50ms |
| Memory usage (10 projects) | < 200MB |
| Binary size | < 30MB |

lines: │  │ PTY Controller  │  │ File Watcher    │  │ IPC Commands        │  │
| **Desktop Runtime** | Tauri | 2.x | Native app shell, Rust backend |
+| **UI Components** | PatternFly | 6.x | Enterprise UI (chat, terminal, logs) |
| **State Management** | @zazcart/toolkit | file:/usr/local/packages/toolkit | State machine + schema validation |
| **PTY Emulation** | portable-pty (Rust) | latest | Process spawning and control |
| **File Watching** | notify (Rust) | latest | Real-time harness file changes |
-- Native Rust backend for PTY control
+PatternFly provides ready-made components for our use cases:
+- **Log viewer** - Real-time log display with ANSI support
**Description:** Open and manage multiple projects simultaneously via tabs.
- Tab context menu (rename, close others, close all)
**Description:** Live output from running processes (Claude Code, servers, etc.)
-- Scrollable log buffer (virtual scroll for performance)
- PTY streams → Tauri events → React state
-**Description:** Display current harness state (progress, features) visually.
**Description:** Open new project from file system.
- Browse button (native file picker via Tauri)
'pty:resize': (processId: string, cols: number, rows: number) => Promise<void>;
-├── CLAUDE.md                  # Project rules for Claude Code
-- Use ring buffer (fixed size array) per process
-- Virtual scroll for rendering (react-window or similar)
-- Use Rust's notify crate (efficient fs events)
-- Stream data via Tauri events (not polling)
-- **No Remote Execution:** No network-based command execution
-- **Sandboxed PTY:** Each PTY in isolated process

# HQ - Harness Orchestrator & Dashboard

## Vision

**HQ** (Headquarters) is a native desktop application that provides a visual dashboard for managing AI agent development sessions. It enables developers to orchestrate, monitor, and control multiple Claude Code sessions across different projects without using a terminal.

## Core Philosophy

> "A Single Pane of Glass for AI-Assisted Development"

HQ transforms the way developers interact with long-running AI agents by providing:
- **Visual Kanban boards** for feature tracking
- **Real-time logs** from all running processes
- **PTY-based process control** with full interactivity
- **Multi-project management** via tabs
- **Harness state visualization**

---

## Architecture Overview

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                         HQ Desktop App                               │
│                    (Tauri + React 18 + BlueprintJS 6)                │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                      │
│  ┌────────────────┐  ┌────────────────┐  ┌────────────────────────┐ │
│  │   Tab Bar      │  │   Kanban View   │  │   Logs Panel           │ │
│  │   (Projects)   │  │   (Features)    │  │   (Real-time PTY)      │ │
│  └────────────────┘  └────────────────┘  └────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                      │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                       State Management Layer                         │
│                  (@zazcart/toolkit state-machine)                    │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                       Rust Backend (Tauri)                           │
│                                                                      │
│  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────┐  │
│  │ PTY Controller  │  │ File Watcher    │  │ IPC Commands        │  │
│  │ (node-pty/rust) │  │ (notify crate)  │  │ (Tauri invoke)      │  │
│  └─────────────────┘  └─────────────────┘  └─────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                      │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                     External Processes                               │
│                                                                      │
│  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────┐  │
│  │ Claude Code #1  │  │ Claude Code #2  │  │ Other Processes     │  │
│  │ (Project A)     │  │ (Project B)     │  │ (servers, tests)    │  │
│  └─────────────────┘  └─────────────────┘  └─────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Technology Stack

| Layer | Technology | Version | Purpose |
|-------|------------|---------|---------|
| **Desktop Runtime** | Tauri | 2.x | Native app shell, Rust backend |
| **Frontend** | React | 18.3.1 | UI framework (BlueprintJS 6 requires React 18) |
| **UI Components** | BlueprintJS | 6.x | Enterprise UI components |
| **State Management** | @zazcart/toolkit | file:/usr/local/packages/toolkit | State machine + schema validation |
| **PTY Emulation** | portable-pty (Rust) | latest | Process spawning and control |
| **File Watching** | notify (Rust) | latest | Real-time harness file changes |
| **IPC** | Tauri Commands | - | Frontend ↔ Backend communication |

### Why These Choices?

**Tauri over Electron:**
- Smaller binary size (~10MB vs ~200MB)
- Native Rust backend for PTY control
- Better security model
- Lower memory footprint

**React 18 (not 19):**
- BlueprintJS 6.x requires React 18
- Stable concurrent features
- Wide ecosystem compatibility

**@zazcart/toolkit:**
- Already proven in production
- Immer-based immutable updates
- Schema validation with typebox
- useSyncExternalStore for React

---

## Feature Specifications

### F001: Multi-Project Tab Management

**Description:** Open and manage multiple projects simultaneously via tabs.

**UI Elements:**
- Tab bar at top (BlueprintJS Tabs)
- New tab button (+)
- Close tab button (X)
- Tab context menu (rename, close others, close all)

**Behavior:**
- Each tab represents a project directory
- Tab title shows project name (from package.json or folder name)
- Active tab is highlighted
- Tabs are reorderable via drag-and-drop

### F002: Kanban Board View

**Description:** Visual kanban board for feature tracking within a project.

**Columns:**
```
BACKLOG → TODO → DOING → REVIEW → QA → DONE
```

**Card Structure:**
- Feature ID (e.g., F001)
- Title
- Description (truncated)
- Status badge
- Priority indicator
- Click to expand details

**Data Source:**
- Reads from `.harness/features.json`
- Auto-updates when file changes (via file watcher)
- Status mapped to columns:
  - `passes: false` + not started → BACKLOG
  - `passes: false` + priority → TODO
  - `passes: false` + in_progress → DOING
  - `passes: false` + needs_review → REVIEW
  - `passes: false` + testing → QA
  - `passes: true` → DONE

### F003: Real-Time Log Panel

**Description:** Live output from running processes (Claude Code, servers, etc.)

**Features:**
- Scrollable log buffer (virtual scroll for performance)
- ANSI color support
- Timestamp prefix
- Process filter (show specific process)
- Search within logs
- Clear button
- Auto-scroll toggle

**Technical:**
- PTY streams → Tauri events → React state
- Max 10,000 lines per process (ring buffer)
- Log persistence to disk (optional)

### F004: PTY Process Controller

**Description:** Spawn, control, and interact with shell processes.

**Capabilities:**
- Start new process (e.g., `claude` command)
- Send keystrokes (including Ctrl+C, Ctrl+D)
- Resize PTY on window resize
- Kill process
- Restart process

**Rust Implementation:**
```rust
use portable_pty::{CommandBuilder, PtySize, native_pty_system};

fn spawn_process(cmd: &str, cols: u16, rows: u16) -> Result<PtyPair> {
    let pty_system = native_pty_system();
    let pair = pty_system.openpty(PtySize { rows, cols, .. })?;
    let cmd = CommandBuilder::new(cmd);
    pair.slave.spawn_command(cmd)?;
    Ok(pair)
}
```

### F005: Harness State Visualization

**Description:** Display current harness state (progress, features) visually.

**Components:**
- Progress summary card
  - Features completed / total
  - Current session info
  - Last commit
- Active feature highlight
- Session timeline (from progress.txt)

**Data Binding:**
- `.harness/progress.txt` → Session history
- `.harness/features.json` → Feature states
- `git log` → Commit history

### F006: Project Selector Dialog

**Description:** Open new project from file system.

**UI:**
- BlueprintJS Dialog
- Recent projects list
- Browse button (native file picker via Tauri)
- Validate project (check for .harness folder)

### F007: Settings Panel

**Description:** Configure HQ preferences.

**Settings:**
- Theme (light/dark/system)
- Log buffer size
- Auto-start Claude Code
- Default shell
- Font size
- PTY configuration

---

## State Schema (using @zazcart/toolkit)

```typescript
import { Type, Static } from 'typebox';
import { createStore, createSlice } from '@zazcart/toolkit';

// Project Schema
const ProjectSchema = Type.Object({
  id: Type.String(),
  path: Type.String(),
  name: Type.String(),
  harness: Type.Optional(Type.Object({
    progressFile: Type.String(),
    featuresFile: Type.String(),
  })),
});

// Feature Schema (from harness)
const FeatureSchema = Type.Object({
  id: Type.String(),
  description: Type.String(),
  category: Type.String(),
  passes: Type.Boolean(),
  steps: Type.Array(Type.String()),
});

// Process Schema
const ProcessSchema = Type.Object({
  id: Type.String(),
  projectId: Type.String(),
  command: Type.String(),
  status: Type.Union([
    Type.Literal('running'),
    Type.Literal('stopped'),
    Type.Literal('error'),
  ]),
  pid: Type.Optional(Type.Number()),
});

// Log Entry Schema
const LogEntrySchema = Type.Object({
  processId: Type.String(),
  timestamp: Type.Number(),
  content: Type.String(),
  level: Type.Union([
    Type.Literal('stdout'),
    Type.Literal('stderr'),
  ]),
});

// App State
const AppStateSchema = Type.Object({
  // UI State
  activeTab: Type.String(),
  theme: Type.Union([
    Type.Literal('light'),
    Type.Literal('dark'),
    Type.Literal('system'),
  ]),

  // Projects
  projects: Type.Record(Type.String(), ProjectSchema),
  projectOrder: Type.Array(Type.String()),

  // Features (per project)
  features: Type.Record(Type.String(), Type.Array(FeatureSchema)),

  // Processes
  processes: Type.Record(Type.String(), ProcessSchema),

  // Logs (ring buffer per process)
  logs: Type.Record(Type.String(), Type.Array(LogEntrySchema)),
});

type AppState = Static<typeof AppStateSchema>;
```

---

## Tauri Commands (IPC)

```typescript
// Frontend → Backend commands

interface TauriCommands {
  // Project management
  'project:open': (path: string) => Promise<Project>;
  'project:close': (id: string) => Promise<void>;
  'project:list_recent': () => Promise<string[]>;

  // Process management
  'pty:spawn': (projectId: string, cmd: string) => Promise<string>;
  'pty:write': (processId: string, data: string) => Promise<void>;
  'pty:resize': (processId: string, cols: number, rows: number) => Promise<void>;
  'pty:kill': (processId: string) => Promise<void>;

  // Harness operations
  'harness:read_features': (path: string) => Promise<Feature[]>;
  'harness:read_progress': (path: string) => Promise<string>;
  'harness:watch': (projectPath: string) => Promise<void>;
  'harness:unwatch': (projectPath: string) => Promise<void>;

  // File operations
  'fs:select_directory': () => Promise<string | null>;
  'fs:read_file': (path: string) => Promise<string>;
}

// Backend → Frontend events
interface TauriEvents {
  'pty:output': { processId: string; data: string };
  'pty:exit': { processId: string; code: number };
  'harness:changed': { projectPath: string; file: string };
}
```

---

## Directory Structure

```
~/antonio/HQ/
├── .claude/
│   ├── settings.json          # Claude hooks config
│   ├── settings.local.json    # Local permissions
│   └── hooks/
│       ├── session-start.sh   # Harness context injection
│       ├── protect-deps.sh    # Block manual package edits
│       └── quality-check.sh   # End-of-turn validation
│
├── .harness/
│   ├── progress.txt           # Session history log
│   └── features.json          # Feature requirements
│
├── docs/
│   ├── HQ_SPEC.md             # This document
│   └── ARCHITECTURE.md        # Technical deep-dive
│
├── src/                       # React frontend
│   ├── main.tsx               # Entry point
│   ├── App.tsx                # Main component
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── TabBar.tsx
│   │   ├── KanbanBoard.tsx
│   │   ├── LogPanel.tsx
│   │   ├── ProcessController.tsx
│   │   └── ProjectSelector.tsx
│   ├── state/
│   │   ├── store.ts           # @zazcart/toolkit store
│   │   ├── slices/
│   │   │   ├── projects.ts
│   │   │   ├── features.ts
│   │   │   ├── processes.ts
│   │   │   └── logs.ts
│   │   └── actions.ts
│   ├── lib/
│   │   ├── tauri.ts           # Tauri command wrappers
│   │   └── pty.ts             # PTY helpers
│   └── views/
│       ├── Dashboard.tsx
│       └── Settings.tsx
│
├── src-tauri/                 # Rust backend
│   ├── Cargo.toml
│   ├── tauri.conf.json
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── main.rs            # Entry point
│   │   ├── commands/
│   │   │   ├── project.rs
│   │   │   ├── pty.rs
│   │   │   ├── harness.rs
│   │   │   └── fs.rs
│   │   └── lib.rs
│   └── icons/
│
├── package.json
├── vite.config.ts
├── tsconfig.json
├── CLAUDE.md                  # Project rules for Claude Code
└── README.md
```

---

## Development Workflow

### Initial Setup

```bash
# 1. Create project with Tauri CLI
cd ~/antonio/HQ
bun create tauri-app --template react-ts

# 2. Add dependencies
bun add react@18 react-dom@18 @blueprintjs/core@6 @blueprintjs/icons@6
bun add "@zazcart/toolkit@file:/usr/local/packages/toolkit"

# 3. Add Rust dependencies (Cargo.toml)
# portable-pty, notify, serde, etc.

# 4. Run development
bun tauri dev
```

### Harness Protocol

1. **Read** `.harness/progress.txt` for session context
2. **Read** `.harness/features.json` for next feature
3. **Implement** one feature at a time
4. **Test** visually with the app running
5. **Commit** after feature passes
6. **Update** `.harness/progress.txt`

---

## Kanban Column Mapping

| Harness State | Kanban Column |
|---------------|---------------|
| `passes: false`, no markers | BACKLOG |
| `passes: false`, `priority: high` | TODO |
| `passes: false`, `status: in_progress` | DOING |
| `passes: false`, `status: review` | REVIEW |
| `passes: false`, `status: testing` | QA |
| `passes: true` | DONE |

**Extended features.json schema:**

```json
{
  "id": "F001",
  "description": "Multi-project tab management",
  "category": "ui",
  "passes": false,
  "priority": "high",
  "status": "in_progress",
  "steps": [
    "Create TabBar component",
    "Implement tab state management",
    "Add new/close tab actions"
  ]
}
```

---

## Performance Considerations

### Log Buffer

- Use ring buffer (fixed size array) per process
- Virtual scroll for rendering (react-window or similar)
- Debounce updates (batch 100ms)

### File Watching

- Use Rust's notify crate (efficient fs events)
- Debounce file change events (500ms)
- Only watch .harness/ directory

### PTY Streaming

- Stream data via Tauri events (not polling)
- Chunk large outputs
- Rate limit to 60fps max

---

## Security Considerations

- **Path Validation:** Validate all project paths
- **Command Sanitization:** Sanitize shell commands
- **No Remote Execution:** No network-based command execution
- **Sandboxed PTY:** Each PTY in isolated process

---

## References

- [Anthropic: Effective Harnesses for Long-Running Agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
- [Anthropic: Building Agents with Claude Agent SDK](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/building-agents-with-the-claude-agent-sdk)
- [Tauri 2.0 Documentation](https://v2.tauri.app/)
- [BlueprintJS 6.x](https://blueprintjs.com/docs/)
- [portable-pty (Rust)](https://docs.rs/portable-pty)

---

## Success Metrics

| Metric | Target |
|--------|--------|
| App startup time | < 2s |
| Log rendering latency | < 16ms (60fps) |
| PTY input latency | < 50ms |
| Memory usage (10 projects) | < 200MB |
| Binary size | < 30MB |
