input.content:
# HQ - Harness Orchestrator & Dashboard

## Project Overview

HQ is a native desktop application (Tauri + React 19 + PatternFly 6) for managing AI agent development sessions. It provides visual Kanban boards, real-time logs, and PTY-based process control for multiple projects.

---

## HARNESS: Fundamental Concept

> **This project uses a Harness.** If you don't know what that is:
> - [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)

### What is a Harness?

A **Harness** is a framework that enables AI agents to work on long tasks across multiple context windows/sessions.

**Problem:** Each new Claude Code session starts with NO memory of previous sessions.

**Solution:** State files persist context between sessions:
- `.harness/progress.txt` → session history
- `.harness/features.json` → requirements with status (passes: true/false)

### Work Protocol

```
1. READ .harness/progress.txt (understand current state)
2. READ .harness/features.json (find next feature with passes: false)
3. IMPLEMENT one feature
4. TEST visually (run the app)
5. COMMIT after passing
6. UPDATE .harness/progress.txt
7. STOP (leave codebase production-ready)
```

---

## Tech Stack

| Technology | Version | Purpose |
|------------|---------|---------|
| **Tauri** | 2.x | Native desktop shell, Rust backend |
| **React** | 19.x | UI framework |
| **PatternFly** | 6.x | Enterprise UI (chat, terminal, logs) |
| **@zazcart/toolkit** | file:/usr/local/packages/toolkit | State machine + schema validation |
| **portable-pty** | latest (Rust) | PTY emulation for process control |
| **notify** | latest (Rust) | File system watching |

### Why PatternFly 6?

- Chat/AI components for agent communication
- LogViewer for real-time log display
- Terminal components
- Tabs for multi-project navigation
- Built-in dark theme support

---

## Project Structure

```
~/antonio/HQ/
├── .claude/              # Claude Code configuration
├── .harness/             # Harness state files
│   ├── progress.txt
│   └── features.json
├── docs/
│   └── HQ_SPEC.md
├── src/                  # React frontend
│   ├── components/
│   ├── state/
│   ├── lib/
│   └── views/
├── src-tauri/            # Rust backend
├── CLAUDE.md
└── package.json
```

---

## State Management

**CRITICAL:** Use `@zazcart/toolkit` for ALL state management.

```typescript
// ✅ CORRECT: Use @zazcart/toolkit
import { createStore, useSelector } from '@zazcart/toolkit';

// ❌ WRONG: React hooks for app state
const [projects, setProjects] = useState([]); // NO
```

---

## Commands

```bash
# Development
bun tauri dev

# Frontend only
bun run dev

# Build
bun tauri build

# Type check
bun run typecheck
```

---

## CRITICAL AI RULES

### Rule 1: CHECK THE CURRENT DATE

```bash
date  # Verify current date
```

### Rule 2: INSTALL DEPENDENCIES VIA COMMANDS

```bash
# CORRECT
bun add react@19 @patternfly/react-core

# WRONG - DO NOT manually edit package.json
```

### Rule 3: VISUAL VERIFICATION

Test visually with Playwright MCP or run the app.

### Rule 4: STOP-SEARCH-UNDERSTAND-APPLY

When stuck (2+ failed attempts): research, don't guess.

---

## Documentation

- `docs/HQ_SPEC.md` - Full system specification
- `.harness/progress.txt` - Session history
- `.harness/features.json` - Feature requirements

# HQ - Harness Orchestrator & Dashboard
# Session Progress Log

================================================================================
SESSION: 2026-01-02 - Initial Setup
================================================================================

## What was done:
- Created project structure at ~/antonio/HQ
- Wrote HQ_SPEC.md with full system specification

- Set up .harness/ with progress.txt and features.json
- Initialized Tauri 2.x project with React 19
- Installed dependencies:
  - react@19.2.3, react-dom@19.2.3

  - @patternfly/react-icons@6.4.0

  - @zazcart/toolkit (linked from /usr/local/packages/toolkit)
  - @tauri-apps/cli@2.9.6, @tauri-apps/api@2.9.1
- Created basic App.tsx with PatternFly components
- Created Rust backend (src-tauri/) with Tauri 2.x setup

## Key decisions:
- Stack: Tauri 2.x + React 19 + PatternFly 6
- State management: @zazcart/toolkit
- PTY: Rust portable-pty crate (to be added)
- File watching: Rust notify crate (to be added)

## Next steps:
- F009: Configure @zazcart/toolkit state store
- F010: Verify Tauri app runs (bun tauri dev)
- F001: Implement multi-project tab management

## Notes:
- PatternFly 6 supports React 17, 18, and 19
- PatternFly has LogViewer, Chat, and Terminal components

================================================================================

# HQ - Harness Orchestrator & Dashboard

A native desktop application for managing AI agent development sessions.

## Stack

- **Tauri 2.x** - Native desktop shell
- **React 19** - UI framework
- **PatternFly 6** - Enterprise UI components
- **@zazcart/toolkit** - State management

## Development

```bash
# Install dependencies
bun install

# Run frontend only
bun run dev

# Run with Tauri (full app)
bun tauri dev

# Build for production
bun tauri build
```

## Harness

This project uses a harness for persistent context across AI sessions.

- `.harness/progress.txt` - Session history
- `.harness/features.json` - Feature requirements

See `docs/HQ_SPEC.md` for full specification.

---

## Project Structure

```
~/antonio/HQ/

│   └── features.json

# HQ - Harness Orchestrator & Dashboard

## Project Overview

HQ is a native desktop application (Tauri + React 18 + BlueprintJS 6) for managing AI agent development sessions. It provides visual Kanban boards, real-time logs, and PTY-based process control for multiple projects.

---

## HARNESS: Fundamental Concept

> **This project uses a Harness.** If you don't know what that is:
> - [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)

### What is a Harness?

A **Harness** is a framework that enables AI agents to work on long tasks across multiple context windows/sessions.

**Problem:** Each new Claude Code session starts with NO memory of previous sessions.

**Solution:** State files persist context between sessions:
- `.harness/progress.txt` → session history
- `.harness/features.json` → requirements with status (passes: true/false)

### Work Protocol

```
1. READ .harness/progress.txt (understand current state)
2. READ .harness/features.json (find next feature with passes: false)
3. IMPLEMENT one feature
4. TEST visually (run the app)
5. COMMIT after passing
6. UPDATE .harness/progress.txt
7. STOP (leave codebase production-ready)
```

---

## Tech Stack

| Technology | Version | Purpose |
|------------|---------|---------|
| **Tauri** | 2.x | Native desktop shell, Rust backend |
| **React** | 18.3.1 | UI framework (required for BlueprintJS 6) |
| **BlueprintJS** | 6.x | Enterprise UI components |
| **@zazcart/toolkit** | file:/usr/local/packages/toolkit | State machine + schema validation |
| **portable-pty** | latest (Rust) | PTY emulation for process control |
| **notify** | latest (Rust) | File system watching |

### Critical Version Notes

- **React 18 ONLY** - BlueprintJS 6 is incompatible with React 19
- **Tauri 2.x** - Use v2 APIs, not v1

---

## Project Structure

```
~/antonio/HQ/
├── .claude/              # Claude Code configuration
│   ├── settings.json     # Hooks configuration
│   └── hooks/            # Hook scripts
├── .harness/             # Harness state files
│   ├── progress.txt      # Session history
│   └── features.json     # Feature requirements
├── docs/
│   └── HQ_SPEC.md        # Full specification
├── src/                  # React frontend
│   ├── components/       # UI components
│   ├── state/            # @zazcart/toolkit store
│   ├── lib/              # Utilities
│   └── views/            # Page views
├── src-tauri/            # Rust backend
│   └── src/              # Tauri commands
├── package.json
├── vite.config.ts
└── CLAUDE.md             # This file
```

---

## State Management

**CRITICAL:** Use `@zazcart/toolkit` for ALL state management.

```typescript
// ✅ CORRECT: Use @zazcart/toolkit
import { createStore, useSelector } from '@zazcart/toolkit';

// ❌ WRONG: React hooks for app state
const [projects, setProjects] = useState([]); // NO
```

The toolkit provides:
- `createStore` - Immer-based state machine
- `useSelector` - React integration (useSyncExternalStore)
- `Type` from typebox - Schema validation

---

## Key Features (Kanban Columns)

```
BACKLOG → TODO → DOING → REVIEW → QA → DONE
```

Features are tracked in `.harness/features.json` with `passes: boolean`.

See `docs/HQ_SPEC.md` for complete feature list.

---

## Commands

```bash
# Development
bun tauri dev

# Build
bun tauri build

# Frontend only (for UI development)
bun run dev

# Type check
bun run typecheck
```

---

## CRITICAL AI RULES

### Rule 1: CHECK THE CURRENT DATE

```bash
date  # Verify current date
```

Your training data is outdated. Always search with current year.

### Rule 2: INSTALL DEPENDENCIES VIA COMMANDS

```bash
# CORRECT
bun add react@18 @blueprintjs/core@6

# WRONG - DO NOT manually edit package.json
```

### Rule 3: VISUAL VERIFICATION

You cannot see the app. Test visually:
1. Run `bun tauri dev`
2. Take screenshots with Playwright MCP
3. Verify UI renders correctly

### Rule 4: STOP-SEARCH-UNDERSTAND-APPLY

When stuck (2+ failed attempts):
1. STOP - Don't guess
2. SEARCH - WebSearch with current year
3. UNDERSTAND - Read documentation
4. APPLY - Use correct solution

---

## Documentation

- `docs/HQ_SPEC.md` - Full system specification
- `.harness/progress.txt` - Session history
- `.harness/features.json` - Feature requirements

---

## Quick Reference

### Tauri Commands (Rust → TypeScript)

```typescript
import { invoke } from '@tauri-apps/api/core';

// Invoke Rust command
const result = await invoke('pty_spawn', { cmd: 'claude' });
```

### BlueprintJS Components

```tsx
import { Button, Card, Tabs, Tag } from '@blueprintjs/core';
import '@blueprintjs/core/lib/css/blueprint.css';
```

### @zazcart/toolkit State

```typescript
import { createStore, Type } from '@zazcart/toolkit';

const store = createStore({
  schema: Type.Object({ ... }),
  initial: { ... },
});
```

1→#!/bin/bash
     2→
     3→# =============================================================================
     4→# init.sh - Harness Initialization Script for MMS System
     5→# =============================================================================
     6→# This script runs at the start of each coding session to:
     7→# 1. Verify the environment is set up correctly
     8→# 2. Install dependencies if needed
     9→# 3. Run type checking
    10→# 4. Optionally start the development server
    11→#
    12→# Usage: ./init.sh [--no-server]
    13→# =============================================================================
    14→
    15→set -e  # Exit on any error
    16→
    17→# Colors for output
    18→RED='\033[0;31m'
    19→GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    20→YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
    21→BLUE='\033[0;34m'
    22→NC='\033[0m' # No Color
    23→
    24→echo "=========================================="
    25→echo "  MMS System - Harness Initialization"
    26→echo "=========================================="
    27→echo ""
    28→
    29→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    30→# Step 1: Check current date (IMPORTANT for AI agents)
    31→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    32→echo -e "${YELLOW}[1/6] Checking current date...${NC}"
    33→CURRENT_DATE=$(date +"%Y-%m-%d")
    34→CURRENT_YEAR=$(date +"%Y")
    35→echo -e "${BLUE}Current date: ${CURRENT_DATE}${NC}"
    36→echo -e "${BLUE}Current year: ${CURRENT_YEAR}${NC}"
    37→echo ""
    38→echo -e "${RED}⚠️  AI REMINDER: Your training data may be outdated.${NC}"
    39→echo -e "${RED}   Include '${CURRENT_YEAR}' in documentation searches.${NC}"
    40→echo -e "${GREEN}✓ Date confirmed${NC}"
    41→echo ""
    42→
    43→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    44→# Step 2: Verify working directory
    45→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    46→echo -e "${YELLOW}[2/6] Verifying working directory...${NC}"
    47→if [ ! -f "CLAUDE.md" ]; then
    48→    echo -e "${RED}ERROR: CLAUDE.md not found. Are you in the project root?${NC}"
    49→    echo "Expected to be in: system/ (MMS monorepo root)"
    50→    exit 1
    51→fi

#!/bin/bash

# =============================================================================
# init.sh - Harness Initialization Script for MMS System
# =============================================================================
# This script runs at the start of each coding session to:
# 1. Verify the environment is set up correctly
# 2. Install dependencies if needed
# 3. Run type checking
# 4. Optionally start the development server
#
# Usage: ./init.sh [--no-server]
# =============================================================================

set -e  # Exit on any error

# Colors for output
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

echo "=========================================="
echo "  MMS System - Harness Initialization"
echo "=========================================="
echo ""

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 1: Check current date (IMPORTANT for AI agents)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo -e "${YELLOW}[1/6] Checking current date...${NC}"
CURRENT_DATE=$(date +"%Y-%m-%d")
CURRENT_YEAR=$(date +"%Y")
echo -e "${BLUE}Current date: ${CURRENT_DATE}${NC}"
echo -e "${BLUE}Current year: ${CURRENT_YEAR}${NC}"
echo ""
echo -e "${RED}⚠️  AI REMINDER: Your training data may be outdated.${NC}"
echo -e "${RED}   Include '${CURRENT_YEAR}' in documentation searches.${NC}"
echo -e "${GREEN}✓ Date confirmed${NC}"
echo ""

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 2: Verify working directory
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo -e "${YELLOW}[2/6] Verifying working directory...${NC}"
if [ ! -f "CLAUDE.md" ]; then
    echo -e "${RED}ERROR: CLAUDE.md not found. Are you in the project root?${NC}"
    echo "Expected to be in: system/ (MMS monorepo root)"
    exit 1
fi

if [ ! -f "package.json" ]; then
    echo -e "${RED}ERROR: package.json not found.${NC}"
    exit 1
fi

echo -e "${GREEN}✓ Working directory confirmed: $(pwd)${NC}"
echo ""

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 3: Check dependencies
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo -e "${YELLOW}[3/6] Checking dependencies...${NC}"

# Check if bun is installed
if ! command -v bun &> /dev/null; then
    echo -e "${RED}ERROR: Bun is not installed.${NC}"
    echo "Install Bun: curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash"
    exit 1
fi

echo "Bun version: $(bun --version)"

# Install dependencies if node_modules doesn't exist or is outdated
if [ ! -d "node_modules" ]; then
    echo "Installing dependencies..."
    bun i
else
    echo "Dependencies already installed."
    echo "Run 'bun i' manually if you need to update."
fi

echo -e "${GREEN}✓ Dependencies verified${NC}"
echo ""

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 4: Run type check
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo -e "${YELLOW}[4/6] Running type check...${NC}"

# Run typecheck but don't fail the script if it errors
if bun run typecheck 2>/dev/null; then
    echo -e "${GREEN}✓ TypeScript types are valid${NC}"
else
    echo -e "${YELLOW}⚠️  TypeScript errors found. Run 'bun run typecheck' for details.${NC}"
fi
echo ""

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 5: Check harness files
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo -e "${YELLOW}[5/6] Checking harness files...${NC}"

HARNESS_FILES=("CLAUDE.md" "claude-progress.txt" "features.json" "CLAUDE_NOTES.md")
MISSING_FILES=()

for file in "${HARNESS_FILES[@]}"; do
    if [ -f "$file" ]; then
        echo "  ✓ $file"
    else
        echo "  ✗ $file (MISSING)"
        MISSING_FILES+=("$file")
    fi
done

if [ -d "agent_docs" ]; then
    echo "  ✓ agent_docs/"
    for doc in agent_docs/*.md; do
        if [ -f "$doc" ]; then
            echo "    ✓ $(basename $doc)"
        fi
    done
else
    echo "  ✗ agent_docs/ (MISSING)"
fi

if [ ${#MISSING_FILES[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
    echo -e "${YELLOW}⚠️  Some harness files are missing.${NC}"
else
    echo -e "${GREEN}✓ All harness files present${NC}"
fi
echo ""

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 6: Display status summary
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo -e "${YELLOW}[6/6] Status summary...${NC}"

# Show git status
echo ""
echo "Git Status:"
git status --short 2>/dev/null || echo "  (not a git repository)"

# Show recent commits
echo ""
echo "Recent commits:"
git --no-pager log --oneline -5 2>/dev/null || echo "  (no commits)"

echo ""
echo "=========================================="
echo -e "${GREEN}  Initialization Complete!${NC}"
echo "=========================================="
echo ""
echo "Next steps:"
echo "  1. Read claude-progress.txt for current state"
echo "  2. Read features.json for next feature to implement"
echo "  3. Start coding!"
echo ""
echo "Useful commands:"
echo "  bun run dev       - Start development server (localhost:5173)"
echo "  bun run build     - Build for production"
echo "  bun run typecheck - Type check all packages"
echo ""

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Optional: Start development server
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
if [ "$1" != "--no-server" ]; then
    echo -e "${YELLOW}Starting development server...${NC}"
    
    # Test if root script works first
    echo "Testing root 'bun run dev' command..."
    if timeout 5 bun run dev 2>&1 | grep -q "No packages matched"; then
        echo -e "${YELLOW}⚠️  Root script failed. Using direct command instead.${NC}"
        echo "Running: cd apps/canvas && bun run dev"
        echo ""
        cd apps/canvas && bun run dev
    else
        echo "Press Ctrl+C to stop the server."
        echo ""
        bun run dev
    fi
fi

set -e  # Exit on any error

# Colors for output

BLUE='\033[0;34m'

echo "=========================================="
echo "  HQ - Harness Initialization"
echo "=========================================="
echo ""

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 1: Check current date (IMPORTANT for AI agents)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo -e "${YELLOW}[1/6] Checking current date...${NC}"
CURRENT_DATE=$(date +"%Y-%m-%d")
CURRENT_YEAR=$(date +"%Y")
echo -e "${BLUE}Current date: ${CURRENT_DATE}${NC}"
echo -e "${BLUE}Current year: ${CURRENT_YEAR}${NC}"
echo ""
echo -e "${RED}⚠️  AI REMINDER: Your training data may be outdated.${NC}"
echo -e "${RED}   Include '${CURRENT_YEAR}' in documentation searches.${NC}"
echo -e "${GREEN}✓ Date confirmed${NC}"
echo ""

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 2: Verify working directory
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo -e "${YELLOW}[2/6] Verifying working directory...${NC}"
if [ ! -f "CLAUDE.md" ]; then
    echo -e "${RED}ERROR: CLAUDE.md not found. Are you in the project root?${NC}"
    echo "Expected to be in: ~/antonio/HQ"
    exit 1
fi

if [ ! -f "package.json" ]; then
    echo -e "${RED}ERROR: package.json not found.${NC}"
    exit 1
fi

echo -e "${GREEN}✓ Working directory confirmed: $(pwd)${NC}"
echo ""

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 3: Check dependencies
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo -e "${YELLOW}[3/6] Checking dependencies...${NC}"

# Check if bun is installed
if ! command -v bun &> /dev/null; then
    echo -e "${RED}ERROR: Bun is not installed.${NC}"
    echo "Install Bun: curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash"
    exit 1
fi

echo "Bun version: $(bun --version)"

# Check if cargo is installed (for Tauri)
if ! command -v cargo &> /dev/null; then
    echo -e "${RED}ERROR: Cargo/Rust is not installed.${NC}"
    echo "Install Rust: curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh"
    exit 1
fi

echo "Cargo version: $(cargo --version)"

# Install dependencies if node_modules doesn't exist
if [ ! -d "node_modules" ]; then
    echo "Installing dependencies..."
    bun i
else
    echo "Dependencies already installed."
    echo "Run 'bun i' manually if you need to update."
fi

echo -e "${GREEN}✓ Dependencies verified${NC}"
echo ""

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 4: Run type check
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo -e "${YELLOW}[4/6] Running type check...${NC}"

if bun run typecheck 2>/dev/null; then
    echo -e "${GREEN}✓ TypeScript types are valid${NC}"
else
    echo -e "${YELLOW}⚠️  TypeScript errors found. Run 'bun run typecheck' for details.${NC}"
fi
echo ""

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 5: Check harness files
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo -e "${YELLOW}[5/6] Checking harness files...${NC}"

HARNESS_FILES=("CLAUDE.md" ".harness/progress.txt" ".harness/features.json")

for file in "${HARNESS_FILES[@]}"; do
    if [ -f "$file" ]; then
        echo "  ✓ $file"
    else
        echo "  ✗ $file (MISSING)"
    fi
done

if [ -d ".claude/hooks" ]; then
    echo "  ✓ .claude/hooks/"
    for hook in .claude/hooks/*.sh; do
        if [ -f "$hook" ]; then
            echo "    ✓ $(basename $hook)"
        fi
    done
fi

if [ -d "src-tauri" ]; then
    echo "  ✓ src-tauri/ (Rust backend)"
fi

echo -e "${GREEN}✓ Harness files checked${NC}"
echo ""

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 6: Display status summary
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo -e "${YELLOW}[6/6] Status summary...${NC}"

# Show git status
echo ""
echo "Git Status:"
git status --short 2>/dev/null || echo "  (not a git repository)"

# Show recent commits
echo ""
echo "Recent commits:"
git --no-pager log --oneline -5 2>/dev/null || echo "  (no commits)"

echo ""
echo "=========================================="
echo -e "${GREEN}  Initialization Complete!${NC}"
echo "=========================================="
echo ""
echo "Next steps:"
echo "  1. Read .harness/progress.txt for current state"
echo "  2. Read .harness/features.json for next feature"
echo "  3. Start coding!"
echo ""
echo "Useful commands:"
echo "  bun run dev       - Start frontend only (localhost:5173)"
echo "  bun tauri dev     - Start full Tauri app"
echo "  bun run typecheck - Type check TypeScript"
echo "  cargo check       - Check Rust code"
echo ""

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Optional: Start development server
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
if [ "$1" != "--no-server" ]; then
    echo -e "${YELLOW}Starting development server (frontend only)...${NC}"
    echo "For full Tauri app, run: bun tauri dev"
    echo "Press Ctrl+C to stop the server."
    echo ""
    bun run dev
fi

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The conversation is summarized below:
Analysis:
Let me analyze the conversation chronologically:

1. **Initial Request**: User wanted to create a new project called "HQ" (Headquarters) - a harness orchestrator and dashboard. The previous project was disorganized, so they wanted a fresh start at ~/antonio/HQ.

2. **Key Requirements Specified**:
   - Dashboard with Kanban (BACKLOG | TODO | DOING | REVIEW | QA | DONE)
   - Real-time logs
   - PTY-based process control for autonomy
   - Use `@zazcart/toolkit` from `/usr/local/packages/toolkit`
   - Native app with Tauri + React + UI framework
   - Multi-project management via tabs
   - Copy hooks and configs from existing project

3. **Research Phase**: I searched for "Anthropic Harness" and learned about:
   - Two-agent architecture (initializer + coding agent)
   - Progress files and feature lists for session persistence
   - Claude Agent SDK concepts

4. **Initial Stack Decision**: Started with React 18 + BlueprintJS 6

5. **User Correction**: User requested switch to React 19 + PatternFly 6, removing all BlueprintJS references

6. **Files Created**:
   - Full project structure at ~/antonio/HQ
   - HQ_SPEC.md, CLAUDE.md, README.md
   - .claude/hooks/ (session-start.sh, protect-deps.sh, quality-check.sh)
   - .harness/progress.txt, features.json
   - src-tauri/ (Cargo.toml, lib.rs, main.rs, tauri.conf.json)
   - src/ (main.tsx, App.tsx, index.css)
   - package.json, vite.config.ts, tsconfig.json

7. **User Questions**:
   - "Foi usado inglês em tudo?" (Was English used throughout?) - Yes
   - "No harness copiou os hooks adaptados?" (Were hooks copied and adapted?) - Yes

8. **User noted modifications**: The system reminded me about user/linter modifications to several files:
   - Changed `@zazcart/toolkit` to `@zazcart/tools`
   - Updated various references accordingly

9. **Final Request**: User asked me to copy and adapt the init.sh script, which I did.

Files read:
- /usr/local/packages/toolkit/package.json
- .claude/hooks/* from MMS project
- .claude/settings.json and settings.local.json from MMS
- init.sh from MMS project

Errors encountered:
- Tauri CLI installation failed initially due to Rust version (needed --locked flag)
- tauri init couldn't run interactively, so I created files manually

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user wanted to create a completely new project called "HQ" (Headquarters) at ~/antonio/HQ. This is a native desktop application for managing AI agent development sessions. Key features requested:
   - Visual Kanban board with columns: BACKLOG | TODO | DOING | REVIEW | QA | DONE
   - Real-time logs from running processes
   - PTY-based process control (using TypeScript PTY emulator or similar)
   - Multi-project management via tabs (like iTerm with Claude Code)
   - Use `@zazcart/tools` package from `/usr/local/packages/toolkit`
   - Copy and adapt .claude/hooks and configurations from the existing MMS project
   - Stack: Tauri 2.x + React 19 + PatternFly 6 (user corrected initial BlueprintJS choice)
   - All documentation in English

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - **Anthropic Harness**: Framework for AI agents to work across multiple context windows. Uses progress.txt and features.json for session persistence
   - **Tauri 2.x**: Native desktop runtime with Rust backend
   - **PatternFly 6**: Enterprise UI components (LogViewer, Chat, Terminal, Tabs)
   - **React 19**: UI framework (PatternFly 6 supports React 17, 18, 19)
   - **@zazcart/tools**: Private state management package with schema validation (typebox)
